messageText:
Hi Quess,

I’m glad you had a chance to connect with Lance. As a next step, we’d like
to bring you in for a work trial.

Could you let us know when you might be available to spend 2-3 days working
with us in the office?

Best,
Dave

On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 2:35 PM Quess Liu <quessliuqes@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Lance,
> Thank you for reaching out.
> I'm available between 11am-2pm PT Friday.
> Kind regards
> Quess Liu
>
> On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 2:35 PM Quess Liu <quessliuqes@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Lance,
>> Thank you for reaching out.
>> I'm available between 11am-2pm PT Friday.
>> Kind regards
>> Quess Liu
>>
>> On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 2:25 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Quess,
>>>
>>> Nice to meet you! I would like to schedule a 30-minute meeting to learn
>>> more about your background.
>>>
>>> I am generally available on weekdays between 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM.
>>> Please let me know which time works best for you, and I will send you a
>>> calendar invitation with a meeting link.
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Lance
>>>
>>> On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 5:53 PM Quess Liu <quessliuqes@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Thank you, looking forward to it!
>>>>
>>>> On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 5:50 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Quess,
>>>>>
>>>>> It was a pleasure speaking with you today. I’m looping in my colleague
>>>>> Lance so the two of you can find a time to connect.
>>>>>
>>>>> Best,
>>>>> Dave
>>>>>
>>>>

 <https://claude.ai/?#headerLogo> 


 Today, we're introducing Claude Opus 4.8 
<https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8?utm_content=link>, our most 
intelligent Opus model and the best generally available model for coding, 
agents, and demanding knowledge work.


 Here's what's new on the Claude Platform (API): 


 * Claude Opus 4.8: The latest version of Opus, with improvements in coding, 
long-running autonomous agents, and reasoning on complex enterprise problems. 
Available at $5 input / $25 output per million tokens. 
 * Fast mode (research preview): Fast mode for Opus 4.8 delivers full Opus 4.8 
intelligence with roughly 2.5x faster output token generation. Fast mode now 
costs less than before, at 2x standard Opus 4.8 pricing. Reach out to your 
account team to get started or request accesshere 
<https://claude.com/fast-mode?utm_content=inline_link>. 
 * Mid-conversation system messages 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/mid-conversation-system-messages?utm_content=inline_link>
: The Messages API now accepts system entries inside the messages array, so you 
can update Claude's instructions mid-task without breaking the prompt cache. 
 * Self-hosted sandboxes for Claude Managed Agents (public beta) 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/self-hosted-sandboxes?utm_content=inline_link>
: Build and deploy agents at scale while keeping sensitive files, packages, and 
services in your own infrastructure or with a managed sandbox provider. 
 * MCP tunnels (research preview) 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/mcp-tunnels/overview?utm_content=inline_link>
: Connect agents to MCP servers inside your private network without exposing 
them to the public internet. Request accesshere 
<https://claude.com/form/claude-managed-agents?utm_content=inline_link>. 


 For Claude Code users: 


 *  Claude Opus 4.8 is now the default fallback model 
<https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config?utm_content=inline_link> in 
Claude Code. It defaults to high effort, which is the best balance of quality 
and speed, costing roughly the same as Opus 4.7 on xhigh effort. 
 * Dynamic workflows 
<https://claude.com/blog/introducing-dynamic-workflows-in-claude-code?utm_content=inline_link>
 are now available in Claude Code, which help Claude tackle extraordinarily 
large tasks, executing across 10s to 100s of parallel subagents. They are on by 
default, and can be toggled off by adding “disableWorkflows: true” to your 
managed settings. See the blog for more details. 



Read the blog 
<https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8?utm_content=hero_cta#ctaButton> 

 Opus 4.8 is available on our API, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and 
Microsoft Foundry.


 To learn more, check out our developer docs 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview?utm_content=inline_link>
,prompt engineering best practices 
<https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/claude-prompting-best-practices?utm_content=inline_link>
, andpricing <https://claude.com/pricing?utm_content=inline_link#api>.

Hey!

Thanks for signing up for Honcho. If you have any questions or want to chat
about what you're building, I'd love to hear from you--feel free to reply here
or join our

[Discord]
https://discord.gg/honcho

.

Otherwise, a star on the

[repo]
https://github.com/plastic-labs/honcho

and follows on

[X/Twitter]
https://x.com/honchodotdev

+

[LinkedIn]
https://www.linkedin.com/company/plasticlabs/

go a long way for us. Appreciate the support and happy building!

Vince Trost

Co-Founder / CEO

Plastic Labs

messageText:
Hello,

We wanted to alert you that your account balance has crossed the $240000.00 threshold you set as the total monthly API spend for the Eragon-Deployment-1 workspace in your organization “Eragon.” Per your configuration, further API usage is now paused for this workspace until midnight UTC on June 1, 2026.

If you'd like to modify your alert settings at any time, you can visit your Alert Settings page in your account dashboard. There, you can remove this limit to immediately re-enable API access for this workspace. Otherwise, API access will be re-enabled at the start of next month.

Warmly,
The Anthropic Team

For any further questions, please visit our help center.
Claude.ai · Research · Products · Company

messageText:
Two code blocks. Pick Python or curl. Done in 30 seconds.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

The fastest path from signup to a working response is two code blocks. Copy one, swap your key, run it. If you already use the OpenAI SDK, the only line that changes is base_url.

Open the quickstart ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=b1_first_request&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

PYTHON

from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI
(   base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
  api_key="YOUR_API_KEY" )


response = client.chat.completions.create
(   model="openai/gpt-4o-mini",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}] )

print(response.choices[0].message.content)

 

CURL

curl https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"openai/gpt-4o-mini","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}]}'

 

What the response looks like. A standard OpenAI chat completion object. choices[0].message.content holds the text. usage has token counts. model shows which model actually served the request, which matters when you use openrouter/auto and want to see what the router picked. If you already read OpenAI responses, you already know how to read these.

If the first call fails. A 401 is almost always the key header. A 402 means the free-tier daily cap was hit or credits are needed for a paid model. A 404 on a model string usually means dots vs hyphens; OpenRouter slugs use dots, so claude-sonnet-4.6 not claude-sonnet-4-6. The models ( https://openrouter.ai/models?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=b1_first_request&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) page has every valid slug.

Free tier: 10 requests per day across 29 free models. No credit card to start.

Getting Started

1. Create your API key ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

2. Point the OpenAI SDK at openrouter.ai/api/v1

3. Make your first request ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=B1-1+First+Response&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

PICK MODELS LATER

Once a single request works, you can let the router choose models for you. openrouter/auto picks per-request based on the prompt. The only change is the model string. Roughly 63 percent of auto traffic routes to models under $1 per million tokens, so simple tasks stop hitting expensive models without any routing code on your side.

response = client.chat.completions.create

messageText:
Hey Lance Mathias,

Ever built an AI agent that can read everything but can't actually do anything?

That's the tool call problem. Your agent can understand "update the deal in Salesforce" but has no hands to make it happen.

Agent Handler gives your AI the ability to take actions on thousands of tools across dozens of connectors. Here's how to make your first one:

Enable your first tool call (5 min) ( https://ah.merge.dev/get-started )

The average developer makes their first authenticated call in under 10 minutes. Here's the steps:

1
Pick a connector

2
Add your credentials

3
Watch your agent take action

- Team Merge

Merge Agent Handler

preview.body: I look forward to talking to you tomorrow PS If you need to re-schedule, you can use this link https://merge.chilipiper.com/reschedule/cf0d80f4-eb01-43ed-af2f-41655c880253 Sent from Chili Piper

subject: Confirming our meeting tomorrow

messageText:
Hi Dave,

Hope you had a good Memorial Day weekend.

Just wanted to quickly follow up on the SOC 2 Type 2 engagement letter I
sent over via DocuSign last Friday.

Let me know if you saw it and if you need anything from me on your side to
move things forward. Once it’s signed, I’ll get you introduced to
onboarding so we can kick off the readiness phase in Vanta.

Best,
Andrew T





*Andrew Topanian*

Account Executive



*E* atopanian@advantage-partners.com

*P** +1 (781)-528-7740*

*Book Time Here <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*


On Fri, May 22, 2026 at 7:41 AM Andrew Topanian <
atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:

> Hi Dave,
>
> Nice to meet you as well.
>
> I just sent the engagement letter for the SOC 2 Type 2 audit to you via
> DocuSign for review/signature.
>
> Once signed, I’ll introduce you to our onboarding team. We will then kick
> off the readiness phase, align in Vanta, and begin preparing everything for
> the audit process and observation window.
>
> Please let me know if any questions come up while reviewing the agreement.
>
> Looking forward to working together.
>
> Best,
> Andrew T
>
>
>
>
>
> *Andrew Topanian*
>
> Account Executive
>
>
>
> *E* atopanian@advantage-partners.com
>
> *P** +1 (781)-528-7740*
>
> *Book Time Here <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*
>
>
> On Fri, May 22, 2026 at 1:55 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Thanks, Lance.
>>
>> Andrew - nice to meet you. Let me know what you need from me in order to
>> proceed.
>>
>> Best,
>> Dave
>>
>>
>> On Thu, May 21, 2026 at 9:28 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Andrew,
>>>
>>> This is our COO Dave who will be taking over SOC2.
>>>
>>> Dave, please see the overview in the email thread.
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Lance
>>>
>>> On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 5:04 PM Andrew Topanian <
>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Lance,
>>>>
>>>> Nice to meet you, and appreciate Kenny looping you in here as the new
>>>> POC for the SOC 2 initiative.
>>>>
>>>> Kenny and I connected last week to walk through Eragon’s current setup
>>>> and what the audit process would look like with Advantage Partners. From
>>>> our conversation, it sounds like the team has already completed the
>>>> readiness work in Vanta and is in a good position to move forward fairly
>>>> quickly.
>>>>
>>>> Quick recap of what we discussed:
>>>>
>>>> - SOC 2 Type 2 audit pricing: $5,000
>>>> - Estimated timeline: ~4–4.5 months total
>>>> - 3-month observation period
>>>> - ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance afterward
>>>>
>>>> We also covered how we approach the process:
>>>> - Readiness review and onboarding assistance included
>>>> - Pre-audit review before the observation period begins
>>>> - Hands-on support throughout the audit so there are no surprises
>>>> during the observation window
>>>> - Tight alignment with Vanta and their workflows, which helps
>>>> streamline the process considerably for lean teams like yours
>>>>
>>>> We additionally discussed optional penetration testing services, and I
>>>> shared black box / gray box examples along with our scoping form:
>>>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>>>
>>>> As another reference point, here’s an example Vanta trust center
>>>> showing what the end result can look like after the audit is complete:
>>>> https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto
>>>>
>>>> At this stage, I mainly wanted to introduce myself and get a sense of
>>>> which direction you’re leaning on the audit side and whether there are any
>>>> remaining questions I can help answer.
>>>>
>>>> Looking forward to working with you.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Andrew T
>>>>
>>>> On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 7:54 PM Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hey Andrew,
>>>>>
>>>>> Lance will be the new POC for our Soc2.
>>>>>
>>>>> On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 11:18 AM Andrew Topanian <
>>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Wanted to follow up again here and see if you’ve had a chance to make
>>>>>> a decision on the SOC 2 audit side.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Based on our conversation, it sounded like you guys were in a strong
>>>>>> position to move fairly quickly, so I just wanted to check whether timing
>>>>>> shifted internally or if there’s anything else you need from me to help
>>>>>> finalize things.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If helpful, I’m also happy to put together the engagement paperwork
>>>>>> so you can review everything internally before making a final call.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>> Andrew T
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *Andrew Topanian*
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Account Executive

>>>>>> On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 2:57 PM Andrew Topanian <
>>>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Just wanted to check in and see where things stand on your end after
>>>>>>> speaking with the other auditor.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Happy to answer any additional questions that came up during your
>>>>>>> evaluation process or clarify anything around timeline, onboarding, or the
>>>>>>> audit itself.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> If it makes sense to move forward, we can get onboarding kicked off
>>>>>>> right away and start preparing your Vanta environment for the audit process.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>> Andrew T
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *[image: image.png]*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Andrew Topanian*

>>>>>>> Account Executive

>>>>>>> <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 6:20 PM Andrew Topanian <
>>>>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Hey Kenny,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Appreciate you jumping on quickly.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Quick recap and a bit more detail on what we discussed:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *SOC 2 Type 2 Audit*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Cost:* $5,000
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Timeline:* ~4–4.5 months total
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>       3-month observation period
>>>>>>>>       -
>>>>>>>>          -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>          ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance
>>>>>>>>          -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>       (Optional expedited reporting: 2–3 weeks for an additional
>>>>>>>>       $1,500)
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *How we run the process*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Onboarding + Vanta access* – we get aligned right away and
>>>>>>>>    support you as you finalize anything in Vanta
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Readiness review (included)* – we help get you fully dialed in
>>>>>>>>    before the audit starts
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Pre-audit review* – we go through everything in detail to
>>>>>>>>    catch and fix anything upfront
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Audit execution* – smooth observation period with minimal
>>>>>>>>    back-and-forth
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> The goal is to make sure you’re not going into the observation
>>>>>>>> window blind and to avoid surprises or findings wherever possible.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Why Advantage vs. other auditors*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    We work extremely closely with Vanta and align tightly with how
>>>>>>>>    their platform is structured
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Heavy focus on prep (readiness + pre-audit), not just
>>>>>>>>    “real-time auditing”
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Hands-on support throughout so you’re not left guessing during
>>>>>>>>    the process
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Built to move quickly and efficiently, especially with a lean
>>>>>>>>    team like yours
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Pen Testing (optional but recommended)*
>>>>>>>> Not required for SOC 2, but it's a strong signal to customers and
>>>>>>>> something we typically recommend completing before the observation window
>>>>>>>> ends.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Black box: starting at $3,000
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Gray box: starting at $4,500
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Scope-based (apps + API endpoints)
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> You can scope that out here:
>>>>>>>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> I’ve also attached:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Black box overview + test example
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Gray box overview + test example
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Example SOC 2 Report / Trust Center*
>>>>>>>> This is a good reference for what your end result will look like
>>>>>>>> and how you can share it with customers:
>>>>>>>> https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> If you scroll down to “Resources,” you’ll see our SOC 2 Type 2
>>>>>>>> report as an example.
>>>>>>>> ------------------------------
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> If you’re leaning toward moving forward, the next step would just
>>>>>>>> be getting you onboarded and granting us access to Vanta so we can kick
>>>>>>>> things off right away.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking or if any questions came up after
>>>>>>>> the call.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>> Andrew T
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *[image: image.png]*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Andrew Topanian*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Account Executive
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *E* atopanian@advantage-partners.com
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *P** +1 (781)-528-7740*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Book Time Here
>>>>>>>> <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*
>>>>>>>>

>>>>>>>>>> Any time works. I have a lot of things lined up tommorow. Can we

>>>>>>>>>> Best,

>>>>>>>>>>

messageText:
Hi Josh,
Since you mentioned Anthropic as a sub-processor, our Data Privacy team
requested for you to add them to your sub-processors list, including
information about how they are used, as well as any other missing
sub-processors. Could you send us an amended version of the DPA?

Thank you!
Guilherme.

subject: Your OpenRouter, Inc receipt [#1433-5192]

messageText:
One API key, 300+ models. Create yours and start making requests.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

Welcome to OpenRouter. You now have one API key that reaches 300+ models across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, DeepSeek, xAI, Mistral, and every other major provider. It is drop-in compatible with the OpenAI SDK, so the integration looks identical to what you already have, and you stop maintaining a separate account and a separate SDK for each provider.

Create your API key ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=welcome&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

One key, one billing surface, one rate limit pool, one place to see usage. When a provider has a bad hour, OpenRouter falls over to the next provider serving the same model, so your app does not cascade failures when OpenAI or Anthropic goes down. When a new model drops, it is a string change, not an integration project.

Getting Started

1. Create your API key ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

2. Point the OpenAI SDK at openrouter.ai/api/v1

3. Make your first request ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart?utm_source=customerio&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=checklist&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

SWITCHING MODELS

Switch from openai/gpt-4o-mini to anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 to openrouter/auto by changing one string in your API call. No new SDK, no new credentials, no second billing account. Browse all 300+ models ( https://openrouter.ai/models?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a1_models_link&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

openrouter/auto is worth knowing from day one. It reads the prompt and picks a model per request, so you can ship without committing to a specific provider. Roughly 63 percent of auto traffic routes to models under $1 per million tokens, which is how simple prompts stop hitting expensive models. See how auto-routing works ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/routers/auto-router?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a1_auto_routing_link&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A1+Welcome&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

lance,

Your company, eragon.ai has partnered with Vanta to stay secure and compliant. You have overdue security tasks to complete in Vanta. Please complete the following tasks so that your company meets their security and compliance commitments.

----
Security tasks

• Install Vanta Device MonitorClick here to complete your tasks (https://app.vanta.com/domain-redirect/69f104d186ec0a142b34372f/?continue=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.vanta.com%2Femployee%2Fonboarding%3Futm_campaign%3DEmployeeDigest%26utm_medium%3Demail%26utm_source%3Doperational)

----
Have a question or need assistance? Send us an email at support@vanta.com

-- Vanta team


Vanta (https://app.vanta.com) | Security and compliance for internet businesses

messageText:
Hello,

We wanted to alert you that your account balance has crossed the $200000.00 threshold you set as the total monthly API spend for the Eragon-Deployment-1 workspace in your organization “Eragon.” Per your configuration, further API usage is now paused for this workspace until midnight UTC on June 1, 2026.

If you'd like to modify your alert settings at any time, you can visit your Alert Settings page in your account dashboard. There, you can remove this limit to immediately re-enable API access for this workspace. Otherwise, API access will be re-enabled at the start of next month.

Warmly,
The Anthropic Team

For any further questions, please visit our help center.
Claude.ai · Research · Products · Company

messageText:
Hi Dave,

Nice to meet you as well.

I just sent the engagement letter for the SOC 2 Type 2 audit to you via
DocuSign for review/signature.

Once signed, I’ll introduce you to our onboarding team. We will then kick
off the readiness phase, align in Vanta, and begin preparing everything for
the audit process and observation window.

Please let me know if any questions come up while reviewing the agreement.

Looking forward to working together.

Best,
Andrew T





*Andrew Topanian*

Account Executive



*E* atopanian@advantage-partners.com

*P** +1 (781)-528-7740*

*Book Time Here <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*


On Fri, May 22, 2026 at 1:55 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Thanks, Lance.
>
> Andrew - nice to meet you. Let me know what you need from me in order to
> proceed.
>
> Best,
> Dave
>
>
> On Thu, May 21, 2026 at 9:28 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Hi Andrew,
>>
>> This is our COO Dave who will be taking over SOC2.
>>
>> Dave, please see the overview in the email thread.
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Lance
>>
>> On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 5:04 PM Andrew Topanian <
>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Lance,
>>>
>>> Nice to meet you, and appreciate Kenny looping you in here as the new
>>> POC for the SOC 2 initiative.
>>>
>>> Kenny and I connected last week to walk through Eragon’s current setup
>>> and what the audit process would look like with Advantage Partners. From
>>> our conversation, it sounds like the team has already completed the
>>> readiness work in Vanta and is in a good position to move forward fairly
>>> quickly.
>>>
>>> Quick recap of what we discussed:
>>>
>>> - SOC 2 Type 2 audit pricing: $5,000
>>> - Estimated timeline: ~4–4.5 months total
>>> - 3-month observation period
>>> - ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance afterward
>>>
>>> We also covered how we approach the process:
>>> - Readiness review and onboarding assistance included
>>> - Pre-audit review before the observation period begins
>>> - Hands-on support throughout the audit so there are no surprises during
>>> the observation window
>>> - Tight alignment with Vanta and their workflows, which helps streamline
>>> the process considerably for lean teams like yours
>>>
>>> We additionally discussed optional penetration testing services, and I
>>> shared black box / gray box examples along with our scoping form:
>>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>>
>>> As another reference point, here’s an example Vanta trust center showing
>>> what the end result can look like after the audit is complete:
>>> https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto
>>>
>>> At this stage, I mainly wanted to introduce myself and get a sense of
>>> which direction you’re leaning on the audit side and whether there are any
>>> remaining questions I can help answer.
>>>
>>> Looking forward to working with you.
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Andrew T
>>>
>>> On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 7:54 PM Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hey Andrew,
>>>>
>>>> Lance will be the new POC for our Soc2.
>>>>
>>>> On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 11:18 AM Andrew Topanian <
>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>>
>>>>> Wanted to follow up again here and see if you’ve had a chance to make
>>>>> a decision on the SOC 2 audit side.
>>>>>
>>>>> Based on our conversation, it sounded like you guys were in a strong
>>>>> position to move fairly quickly, so I just wanted to check whether timing
>>>>> shifted internally or if there’s anything else you need from me to help
>>>>> finalize things.
>>>>>
>>>>> If helpful, I’m also happy to put together the engagement paperwork so
>>>>> you can review everything internally before making a final call.
>>>>>
>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking.
>>>>>
>>>>> Best,
>>>>> Andrew T
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> *Andrew Topanian*
>>>>>
>>>>> Account Executive
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> *E* atopanian@advantage-partners.com
>>>>>
>>>>> *P** +1 (781)-528-7740*
>>>>>
>>>>> *Book Time Here
>>>>> <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 2:57 PM Andrew Topanian <
>>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Just wanted to check in and see where things stand on your end after
>>>>>> speaking with the other auditor.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Happy to answer any additional questions that came up during your
>>>>>> evaluation process or clarify anything around timeline, onboarding, or the
>>>>>> audit itself.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If it makes sense to move forward, we can get onboarding kicked off
>>>>>> right away and start preparing your Vanta environment for the audit process.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>> Andrew T
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *[image: image.png]*
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *Andrew Topanian*
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Account Executive
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *E* atopanian@advantage-partners.com
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *P** +1 (781)-528-7740*
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *Book Time Here
>>>>>> <https://calendly.com/atopanian-advantage-partners/30min>*
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 6:20 PM Andrew Topanian <
>>>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hey Kenny,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Appreciate you jumping on quickly.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Quick recap and a bit more detail on what we discussed:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *SOC 2 Type 2 Audit*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    *Cost:* $5,000
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    *Timeline:* ~4–4.5 months total
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>       3-month observation period
>>>>>>>       -
>>>>>>>          -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>          ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance
>>>>>>>          -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>       (Optional expedited reporting: 2–3 weeks for an additional
>>>>>>>       $1,500)
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *How we run the process*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    *Onboarding + Vanta access* – we get aligned right away and
>>>>>>>    support you as you finalize anything in Vanta
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    *Readiness review (included)* – we help get you fully dialed in
>>>>>>>    before the audit starts
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    *Pre-audit review* – we go through everything in detail to catch
>>>>>>>    and fix anything upfront
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    *Audit execution* – smooth observation period with minimal
>>>>>>>    back-and-forth
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> The goal is to make sure you’re not going into the observation
>>>>>>> window blind and to avoid surprises or findings wherever possible.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Why Advantage vs. other auditors*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    We work extremely closely with Vanta and align tightly with how
>>>>>>>    their platform is structured
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Heavy focus on prep (readiness + pre-audit), not just “real-time
>>>>>>>    auditing”
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Hands-on support throughout so you’re not left guessing during
>>>>>>>    the process
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Built to move quickly and efficiently, especially with a lean
>>>>>>>    team like yours
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Pen Testing (optional but recommended)*
>>>>>>> Not required for SOC 2, but it's a strong signal to customers and
>>>>>>> something we typically recommend completing before the observation window
>>>>>>> ends.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Black box: starting at $3,000
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Gray box: starting at $4,500
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Scope-based (apps + API endpoints)
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> You can scope that out here:
>>>>>>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> I’ve also attached:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Black box overview + test example
>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    Gray box overview + test example
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Example SOC 2 Report / Trust Center*

preview.body: Hi Dave, Nice to meet you as well. I just sent the engagement letter for the SOC 2 Type 2 audit to you via DocuSign for review/signature. Once signed, I'll introduce you to our onboarding team. We
preview.subject: Re: Introduction: Advantage + Eragon

subject: Re: Introduction: Advantage + Eragon

messageText:
Thanks, Lance.

Andrew - nice to meet you. Let me know what you need from me in order to
proceed.

Best,
Dave


On Thu, May 21, 2026 at 9:28 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Andrew,
>
> This is our COO Dave who will be taking over SOC2.
>
> Dave, please see the overview in the email thread.
>
> Thanks,
> Lance
>
> On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 5:04 PM Andrew Topanian <
> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Lance,
>>
>> Nice to meet you, and appreciate Kenny looping you in here as the new POC
>> for the SOC 2 initiative.
>>
>> Kenny and I connected last week to walk through Eragon’s current setup
>> and what the audit process would look like with Advantage Partners. From
>> our conversation, it sounds like the team has already completed the
>> readiness work in Vanta and is in a good position to move forward fairly
>> quickly.
>>
>> Quick recap of what we discussed:
>>
>> - SOC 2 Type 2 audit pricing: $5,000
>> - Estimated timeline: ~4–4.5 months total
>> - 3-month observation period
>> - ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance afterward
>>
>> We also covered how we approach the process:
>> - Readiness review and onboarding assistance included
>> - Pre-audit review before the observation period begins
>> - Hands-on support throughout the audit so there are no surprises during
>> the observation window
>> - Tight alignment with Vanta and their workflows, which helps streamline
>> the process considerably for lean teams like yours
>>
>> We additionally discussed optional penetration testing services, and I
>> shared black box / gray box examples along with our scoping form:
>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>
>> As another reference point, here’s an example Vanta trust center showing
>> what the end result can look like after the audit is complete:
>> https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto
>>
>> At this stage, I mainly wanted to introduce myself and get a sense of
>> which direction you’re leaning on the audit side and whether there are any
>> remaining questions I can help answer.
>>
>> Looking forward to working with you.
>>
>> Best,
>> Andrew T
>>
>> On Tue, May 19, 2026 at 7:54 PM Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hey Andrew,
>>>
>>> Lance will be the new POC for our Soc2.

>>>>

>>>> decision on the SOC 2 audit side.
>>>>
>>>> Based on our conversation, it sounded like you guys were in a strong
>>>> position to move fairly quickly, so I just wanted to check whether timing
>>>> shifted internally or if there’s anything else you need from me to help
>>>> finalize things.
>>>>
>>>> If helpful, I’m also happy to put together the engagement paperwork so
>>>> you can review everything internally before making a final call.
>>>>
>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Andrew T

>>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>>
>>>>> Just wanted to check in and see where things stand on your end after
>>>>> speaking with the other auditor.
>>>>>
>>>>> Happy to answer any additional questions that came up during your
>>>>> evaluation process or clarify anything around timeline, onboarding, or the
>>>>> audit itself.
>>>>>
>>>>> If it makes sense to move forward, we can get onboarding kicked off
>>>>> right away and start preparing your Vanta environment for the audit process.
>>>>>
>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking.
>>>>>
>>>>> Best,

>>>>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 6:20 PM Andrew Topanian <
>>>>> atopanian@advantage-partners.com> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Hey Kenny,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Appreciate you jumping on quickly.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Quick recap and a bit more detail on what we discussed:
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *SOC 2 Type 2 Audit*
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    *Cost:* $5,000
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    *Timeline:* ~4–4.5 months total
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>       3-month observation period
>>>>>>       -
>>>>>>          -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>          ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance
>>>>>>          -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>       (Optional expedited reporting: 2–3 weeks for an additional
>>>>>>       $1,500)
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *How we run the process*
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    *Onboarding + Vanta access* – we get aligned right away and
>>>>>>    support you as you finalize anything in Vanta
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    *Readiness review (included)* – we help get you fully dialed in
>>>>>>    before the audit starts
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    *Pre-audit review* – we go through everything in detail to catch
>>>>>>    and fix anything upfront
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    *Audit execution* – smooth observation period with minimal
>>>>>>    back-and-forth
>>>>>>
>>>>>> The goal is to make sure you’re not going into the observation window
>>>>>> blind and to avoid surprises or findings wherever possible.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *Why Advantage vs. other auditors*
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    We work extremely closely with Vanta and align tightly with how
>>>>>>    their platform is structured
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Heavy focus on prep (readiness + pre-audit), not just “real-time
>>>>>>    auditing”
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Hands-on support throughout so you’re not left guessing during
>>>>>>    the process
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Built to move quickly and efficiently, especially with a lean
>>>>>>    team like yours
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *Pen Testing (optional but recommended)*
>>>>>> Not required for SOC 2, but it's a strong signal to customers and
>>>>>> something we typically recommend completing before the observation window
>>>>>> ends.
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Black box: starting at $3,000
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Gray box: starting at $4,500
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Scope-based (apps + API endpoints)
>>>>>>
>>>>>> You can scope that out here:
>>>>>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I’ve also attached:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Black box overview + test example
>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>
>>>>>>    Gray box overview + test example
>>>>>>
>>>>>> *Example SOC 2 Report / Trust Center*
>>>>>> This is a good reference for what your end result will look like and
>>>>>> how you can share it with customers:
>>>>>> https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If you scroll down to “Resources,” you’ll see our SOC 2 Type 2 report
>>>>>> as an example.
>>>>>> ------------------------------
>>>>>>
>>>>>> If you’re leaning toward moving forward, the next step would just be
>>>>>> getting you onboarded and granting us access to Vanta so we can kick things
>>>>>> off right away.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Let me know what you’re thinking or if any questions came up after
>>>>>> the call.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>> Andrew T
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>

preview.subject: Re: Introduction: Advantage + Eragon

subject: Re: Introduction: Advantage + Eragon

messageText:
Hi Lance,

Nice to meet you, and appreciate Kenny looping you in here as the new POC
for the SOC 2 initiative.

Kenny and I connected last week to walk through Eragon’s current setup and
what the audit process would look like with Advantage Partners. From our
conversation, it sounds like the team has already completed the readiness
work in Vanta and is in a good position to move forward fairly quickly.

Quick recap of what we discussed:

- SOC 2 Type 2 audit pricing: $5,000
- Estimated timeline: ~4–4.5 months total
- 3-month observation period
- ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance afterward

We also covered how we approach the process:
- Readiness review and onboarding assistance included
- Pre-audit review before the observation period begins
- Hands-on support throughout the audit so there are no surprises during
the observation window
- Tight alignment with Vanta and their workflows, which helps streamline
the process considerably for lean teams like yours

We additionally discussed optional penetration testing services, and I
shared black box / gray box examples along with our scoping form:
https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page

As another reference point, here’s an example Vanta trust center showing
what the end result can look like after the audit is complete:
https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto

At this stage, I mainly wanted to introduce myself and get a sense of which
direction you’re leaning on the audit side and whether there are any
remaining questions I can help answer.

Looking forward to working with you.

Best,
Andrew T

>>>> Hey Kenny,
>>>>
>>>> Appreciate you jumping on quickly.
>>>>
>>>> Quick recap and a bit more detail on what we discussed:
>>>>
>>>> *SOC 2 Type 2 Audit*
>>>>
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    *Cost:* $5,000
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    *Timeline:* ~4–4.5 months total
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>       3-month observation period
>>>>       -
>>>>          -
>>>>
>>>>          ~4–6 weeks for testing, reporting, and issuance
>>>>          -
>>>>
>>>>       (Optional expedited reporting: 2–3 weeks for an additional
>>>>       $1,500)
>>>>
>>>> *How we run the process*
>>>>
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    *Onboarding + Vanta access* – we get aligned right away and support
>>>>    you as you finalize anything in Vanta
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    *Readiness review (included)* – we help get you fully dialed in
>>>>    before the audit starts
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    *Pre-audit review* – we go through everything in detail to catch
>>>>    and fix anything upfront
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    *Audit execution* – smooth observation period with minimal
>>>>    back-and-forth
>>>>
>>>> The goal is to make sure you’re not going into the observation window
>>>> blind and to avoid surprises or findings wherever possible.
>>>>
>>>> *Why Advantage vs. other auditors*
>>>>
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    We work extremely closely with Vanta and align tightly with how
>>>>    their platform is structured
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Heavy focus on prep (readiness + pre-audit), not just “real-time
>>>>    auditing”
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Hands-on support throughout so you’re not left guessing during the
>>>>    process
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Built to move quickly and efficiently, especially with a lean team
>>>>    like yours
>>>>
>>>> *Pen Testing (optional but recommended)*
>>>> Not required for SOC 2, but it's a strong signal to customers and
>>>> something we typically recommend completing before the observation window
>>>> ends.
>>>>
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Black box: starting at $3,000
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Gray box: starting at $4,500
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Scope-based (apps + API endpoints)
>>>>
>>>> You can scope that out here:
>>>> https://ap-pentest-scoping.zapier.app/page
>>>>
>>>> I’ve also attached:
>>>>
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Black box overview + test example
>>>>    -
>>>>
>>>>    Gray box overview + test example
>>>>
>>>> *Example SOC 2 Report / Trust Center*

>>>> https://app.vanta.com/dust.tt/trust/f3ytzxpay31bwsiyuqjto
>>>>
>>>> If you scroll down to “Resources,” you’ll see our SOC 2 Type 2 report
>>>> as an example.
>>>> ------------------------------
>>>>
>>>> If you’re leaning toward moving forward, the next step would just be

>>>>

>>> Quick recap and a bit more detail on what we discussed:

>>> *SOC 2 Type 2 Audit*

>>>    -

>>>

>>>

>>>    you as you finalize anything in Vanta

>>>    -
>>>

>>>    fix anything upfront
>>>    -
>>>
>>>    *Audit execution* – smooth observation period with minimal
>>>    back-and-forth
>>>
>>> The goal is to make sure you’re not going into the observation window
>>> blind and to avoid surprises or findings wherever possible.
>>>
>>> *Why Advantage vs. other auditors*
>>>

>>>

>>>    -

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Lauren Griffin <lauren.griffin@vanta.com>
Date: Wed, Apr 29, 2026 at 5:55 PM
Subject: Introduction: Advantage + Eragon
To: <kenny@eragon.ai>, <atopanian@advantage-partners.com>
Cc: Samir Malhotra <samir.malhotra@vanta.com>


Hey Andrew,

I wanted to take a minute to introduce you to a new Vanta client, Kenny at
Eragon. The Eragon team hopes to achieve SOC2 compliance soon and wants to
understand Advantage's role in the audit process.

They recently completed all the readiness tasks in Vanta and are looking
for a partner to take on next steps.

I’ll let you all take it from here!


*Lauren Griffin*

Account Executive

Book time with me here
<https://vanta.my.leandata.com/l/7bnnqYM/lauren-griffin-30-minute-meeting>

These issues require
your immediate attention

We detected security vulnerabilities in the projects listed below that could expose your data to unauthorized access. Review and fix them before your data is compromised.


Issues as of 03 May 2026:



Critical issue

Sensitive data publicly accessible

A table with columns that likely contain sensitive data (like passwords or personal identifiers) is accessible through the API without any access restrictions.

sensitive_columns_exposed


Project

Eragon-Platform
https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/frylqlbgtrpmqeguwjyq/advisors/security
frylqlbgtrpmqeguwjyq





Critical issue

Table publicly accessible

Anyone with your project URL can read, edit, and delete all data in this table because Row-Level Security is not enabled.

rls_disabled_in_public


Project

Eragon-Platform
https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/frylqlbgtrpmqeguwjyq/advisors/security
frylqlbgtrpmqeguwjyq





If these are not intentional, they could result in unauthorized access to your database. We have a robust set of security checks which you can read about in our docs https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/database-advisors.

Reach out to our support team https://supabase.help if you have any questions.

Regards,
Supabase

Prefer not to open links from emails? Sign in to Supabase, open an affected project, then navigate to Advisors from the sidebar.

----------------------------------------
Supabase Inc, 3500 S. DuPont Highway, Kent 19901, Dover, Delaware, USA

messageText:
Quess Liu Work Trial
Tuesday Jun 2 – Thursday Jun 4, 2026



Quess Liu is a Senior AI/ML Engineer with 13+ years of experience building  
and deploying large-scale machine learning, generative AI, and  
reinforcement learning systems, with deep expertise in LLMs, RLHF, RAG  
pipelines, and MLOps.

Most recently at TogetherAI, they led the design of advanced AI platforms  
including Model Context Protocol (MCP), multi-agent systems, and  
enterprise-grade LLM applications, improving retrieval accuracy, reducing  
hallucinations, and optimizing latency and cost.

Organizer
Dave Anderson
dave@eragon.ai

Guests
Dave Anderson - organizer
Josh Sirota
Lance Mathias
Nick Adesina
aaron@eragon.ai
View all guest info  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=MDNhc3NoZGFtMHY1Mm8yaWQycjMwMHU5cWogbGFuY2VAZXJhZ29uLmFp&tok=MTQjZGF2ZUBlcmFnb24uYWkxNWFhZjc5NjM3NTk0YmY2YmM5ODZlYzIxNjdiNTAzZmM0Yjk0ZmE1&ctz=America%2FLos_Angeles&hl=en&es=1

Reply for lance@eragon.ai and view more details  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=MDNhc3NoZGFtMHY1Mm8yaWQycjMwMHU5cWogbGFuY2VAZXJhZ29uLmFp&tok=MTQjZGF2ZUBlcmFnb24uYWkxNWFhZjc5NjM3NTk0YmY2YmM5ODZlYzIxNjdiNTAzZmM0Yjk0ZmE1&ctz=America%2FLos_Angeles&hl=en&es=1
Your attendance is optional.

~~//~~
Invitation from Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/

You are receiving this email because you are subscribed to calendar  
notifications. To stop receiving these emails, go to  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/r/settings, select this calendar, and  
change "Other notifications".

Forwarding this invitation could allow any recipient to send a response to  
the organizer, be added to the guest list, invite others regardless of  
their own invitation status, or modify your RSVP.

Learn more https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/37135#forwarding

messageText:
Analyse zoominfo vendor contract. Start by searching my Gmail (last 90
days) and Slack for any conversations involving this vendor: extract
pricing discussed, commitments made, and internal sentiment. Then extract
all commercial terms from the contract (TCV, payment terms, auto-renewal,
price cap). Check compliance against our 3 non-negotiables: auto-renewal
opt-out, minimum Net 30 payment terms, and renewal price cap ≤3% or CPI.
Flag any items requiring Legal review. Then give me a prioritised
negotiation strategy with 3–5 levers: specific asks, rationale, and
estimated impact, informed by what you found in Gmail and Slack. Close with
a Final Verdict: Approved, Approved with Conditions, or Blocked.

On Thu, May 28, 2026 at 12:44 AM Olivier Elbaz <olivier@deel.com> wrote:

> + Graham
> + Orr
>
> Graham, Orr,
>
> Meet Rishabh and Lance from the tech team at Eragon (Rishabh is by the way
> co-founder and CTO).
>
> Can you share some mid to complex prompt you or your team are doing on a
> daily basis with Claude Co-work?
> We would like to evaluate how eragon can perform the same level of quality
> output vs Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini /… while showing a lower cost of
> token consumption and ultimately reduce $ spent with Frontier model.
>
> Thanks,
>
>
> Olivier Elbaz
> Global Head of Expansion
> m: +972 58 780 55 76
> www.letsdeel.com
>
>
> Le jeu. 28 mai 2026 à 00:03, Rishabh Tiwari <rishabh@eragon.ai> a écrit :
>
>> "First email from (rishabh@eragon.ai). Be cautious
>> Hi Olivier,
>>
>> Quick follow up on the sample workflows, Lance is amazing, he has already
>> prepared the eval pipeline that we discussed yesterday, we are now just
>> waiting to run it on workflows that are most relevant to Deel. Pls send the
>> sample workflows at your earliest convenience.
>>
>> Thanks and regards
>> Rishabh
>>
>> On Tue, May 26, 2026 at 3:11 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Olivier,
>>>
>>> We’re currently doing some testing on the Eragon routing and we’d love
>>> to do some testing to measure the improvement that our routing system
>>> provides on the type of work that you guys do.
>>>
>>> Can you please send us 4-5 example workflows that your team at Deel use
>>> regularly?
>>>
>>> Thanks!
>>> Lance
>>>
>>

-- 
Orr Zilcha
Director of Procurement - Deel

Friday May 29, 2026 ⋅ 9am – 9:20am
Pacific Time - Los Angeles

Join with Google Meet
https://meet.google.com/oob-hrmz-srf?hs=224


	
Join by phone
(US) +1 857-816-9049
PIN: 337395409

More phone numbers
https://tel.meet/oob-hrmz-srf?pin=4738180333881&hs=0


Quick 20-minute call to walk through your $10K Eragon credit activation and  
get everything set up.

Organizer
Josh Sirota
josh@eragon.ai

Guests
Josh Sirota - organizer
Lance Mathias
bronwyn@pragmaclin.com

messageText:
JSON Schema enforcement at decode time, across 244 of 357 catalog models.  

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai?utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

*******************************************************************************************************
One response_format schema, enforced at decode time across 244 of 357 models, refused on the other 113.
*******************************************************************************************************

Ask a model for JSON and you often get prose or malformed output, so the schema ends up living in prompt-and-retry loops and the repair code that catches what retry misses. Send a response_format with a JSON Schema and OpenRouter enforces it at decode time across 244 of 357 catalog models.

Switch the model slug and the schema contract holds: form auto-fill and entity extraction, classification and routing decisions, agent state machines and function arguments all parse the same way.

{
"messages": [
{ "role": "user", "content": "What's the weather like in London?" }
], "response_format": { "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": { "name": "weather", "strict": true, "schema": { "type": "object", "properties": { "location": { "type": "string", "description": "City or location name"
}, "temperature": { "type": "number", "description": "Temperature in Celsius"
}, "conditions": { "type": "string", "description": "Weather conditions description"
}
}, "required": ["location", "temperature", "conditions"], "additionalProperties": false
}
}
}
}

Read the structured outputs guide ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/structured-outputs?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

-----------------------
Pairs with tool calling
-----------------------

Tool calling ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/tool-calling?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured_support_a&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) uses the same one-parameter pattern as structured output. Pass a tools array, the model returns conforming JSON, no wrapper code. Same model slug, same endpoint.

The headless agent recipe ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/cookbook/building-agents/create-headless-agent?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured_support_b&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) wires both into a production loop that runs without a UI. Structured output for the model's response shape, tool calling for the actions in between.

Read the headless agent recipe ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/cookbook/building-agents/create-headless-agent?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_structured_secondary_cta&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Latest from the blog

Human-in-the-Loop Tools for the Agent SDK ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/human-in-the-loop-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_hitl_blog_1&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Human-in-the-Loop Tools for the Agent SDK ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/human-in-the-loop-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_hitl_blog_1&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
A new tool type that lets agents auto-resolve routine decisions and pause for human input on critical ones.

Read the announcement → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/human-in-the-loop-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_hitl_blog_1&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Consistent web search and fetch across every model ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/agentic-web-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_web_blog_2&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Consistent web search and fetch across every model ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/agentic-web-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_web_blog_2&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
Tool-calling models get web search and page-fetching, normalized across providers.

See the tools → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/agentic-web-tools?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_web_blog_2&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Response caching: zero cost for identical requests ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/response-caching?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_cache_blog_3&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Response caching: zero cost for identical requests ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/response-caching?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_cache_blog_3&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
Cache identical requests at zero token cost with one header. Dramatically faster, free.

Read the launch → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements/response-caching?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_cache_blog_3&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

More shipping soon. Full changelog lives at openrouter.ai/announcements. View announcements → ( https://openrouter.ai/announcements?utm_campaign=reactivation_rot_v1&utm_source=lifecycle&utm_medium=email&utm_content=m6_blog_view&utm_campaign=Reactivation+Rotational+Test+May%2726&utm_content=Day+0+%2F+M6+Structured+Outputs&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Thursday May 28, 2026 ⋅ 12pm – 12:20pm
Pacific Time - Los Angeles

Join with Google Meet
https://meet.google.com/xzb-iboc-iih?hs=224


	
Join by phone
(US) +1 614-948-6208
PIN: 642467575

More phone numbers
https://tel.meet/xzb-iboc-iih?pin=9695787739866&hs=0


Quick 20-minute call to walk through your $10K Eragon credit activation and  
get everything set up.

Organizer
Josh Sirota
josh@eragon.ai

Josh Sirota - organizer

brian.faulkner@playante.net

 <https://claude.ai/?#headerLogo> 


 Hello, 


 Fast mode for Claude Opus 4.8 is now available in research preview on Claude 
Code. It delivers full Opus 4.8 intelligence at ~2.5x output speed for 2x the 
cost, which is one-third the price of fast mode on previous models. Going 
forward, running /fast in Claude Code will now default to Opus 4.8.


Fast mode for Opus 4.6 will be retired on June 29th, 2026. After that date, 
fast mode requests for Opus 4.6 will run at standard speed and regular pricing. 


 This retirement applies only to fast mode for Opus 4.6. You can continue 
using Opus 4.6 at standard speed.


 Explore the documentation 
<https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fast-mode?utm_content=inline_link> to learn 
more.



 <https://claude.ai/?#footerLogo> 

 <https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/claude/?#footerLinkedIn>  
<https://x.com/claudeai?#footerX>  
<https://www.youtube.com/@anthropic-ai?#footerYoutube> 



 Anthropic PBC, 54‌8 M‌a‌r‌k‌e‌t S‌t, PMB 9‌0‌3‌7‌5, Sa‌n F‌r‌a‌n‌c‌i‌s‌c‌o, 
C‌A 9‌4‌1‌0‌4 


 This email was sent to lance@eragon.ai.

messageText:
Hi Lance Mathias,
 
Welcome to Langfuse, glad that you signed up!
 
I'm Marc, one of the founders and here to help in case you have any questions.
 
Langfuse has three core pillars:
 * Observability – trace every LLM call, chain steps and cost on one timeline.

 * Prompt management – version‑control prompts and roll them out without redeploys.

 * Evaluations – auto‑ or human‑grade results once you have traffic to benchmark.

 
Watch a short intro: Youtube link [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zzOlFH0iD0k&list=PL4v4zZF1rGG6Bmt3dgtQc31Byzy97t0xr]

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zzOlFH0iD0k&list=PL4v4zZF1rGG6Bmt3dgtQc31Byzy97t0xr

 
Or check out the interactive demo [https://langfuse.com/docs/demo] (you have access in your account) and play with the chatbot to
see your traces in the app
 
Start by using AI Agents
 
Just copy these prompts to let your coding agent do the work:
 * Tracing: ‘Please follow the instructions on this page to set up tracing for my project:
   https://langfuse.com/docs/observability/get-started.md
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/observability/get-started.md?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]’ leveraging the Langfuse
   skill https://github.com/langfuse/skills [https://github.com/langfuse/skills]'

 * Prompt Management: ‘Please follow the instructions on this page to set up prompt management for my project:
   https://langfuse.com/docs/prompt-management/get-started.md
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/prompt-management/get-started.md?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]leveraging the Langfuse
   skill https://github.com/langfuse/skills [https://github.com/langfuse/skills]'

 
Start with manual Setup
 * Quickstart to start tracing your application
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/observability/get-started?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]

 * Quickstart to get started with prompt management
   [https://langfuse.com/docs/prompt-management/get-started?ref=onboarding_emails__1_platform_overview]

 
Need help? Just hit reply, I read every message or book a call with an engineer
[https://cal.com/team/langfuse/welcome-to-langfuse] to chat about your use case.
 
Be part of the open-source community:
 * Vote on community submissions and add your own ideas on our Github Discussions
   [https://github.com/orgs/langfuse/discussions/categories/ideas]

 * Check out the roadmap [https://langfuse.com/docs/roadmap]

 * If you like our work, consider leaving a star ⭐ [https://github.com/langfuse/langfuse]

 * Join our weekly Virtual Community Hours [https://luma.com/langfuse?]

 
--
Marc Klingen
Co-founder & CEO, Langfuse

[https://langfuse.com/?utm_source=transactional&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=welcome_to_langfuse_cloud]@marcklingen
[https://x.com/marcklingen]
 
You are receiving this email because you opted in to receive email from the list Onboarding Journey from Langfuse
Langfuse, Langfuse GmbH, Oranienburger Strasse 91, 10178, Berlin, Germany
Unsubscribe [***REDACTED***

messageText:
This event has been updated
Changed: conferencing


Eragon Credit Activation - Deep Sachan
Thursday May 28, 2026 ⋅ 11am – 11:20am
Pacific Time - Los Angeles

Join with Google Meet
https://meet.google.com/wgc-tfvh-urb?hs=224


	
Join by phone
(US) +1 240-532-3609
PIN: 466020209

More phone numbers
https://tel.meet/wgc-tfvh-urb?pin=2992266443388&hs=0


Quick 20-minute call to walk through your $10K Eragon credit activation and  
get everything set up.

Organizer
Josh Sirota
josh@eragon.ai

Guests
Josh Sirota - organizer
Lance Mathias
deep@zweet.io
View all guest info  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=NTRpNDZpZGs3cjhhMzNscnBoNzVtNzZybW8gbGFuY2VAZXJhZ29uLmFp&tok=MTQjam9zaEBlcmFnb24uYWk1MGE4MWM3NTgyOTE4NzAxOTIxY2RkZGIyYzk0MDE1NzRjYjZkYTM5&ctz=America%2FLos_Angeles&hl=en&es=1

Reply for lance@eragon.ai and view more details  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=NTRpNDZpZGs3cjhhMzNscnBoNzVtNzZybW8gbGFuY2VAZXJhZ29uLmFp&tok=MTQjam9zaEBlcmFnb24uYWk1MGE4MWM3NTgyOTE4NzAxOTIxY2RkZGIyYzk0MDE1NzRjYjZkYTM5&ctz=America%2FLos_Angeles&hl=en&es=1
Your attendance is optional.

~~//~~
Invitation from Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/

You are receiving this email because you are subscribed to calendar  
notifications. To stop receiving these emails, go to  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/r/settings, select this calendar, and  
change "Other notifications".

Forwarding this invitation could allow any recipient to send a response to  
the organizer, be added to the guest list, invite others regardless of  
their own invitation status, or modify your RSVP.

Learn more https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/37135#forwarding

subject: Re: Invitation: Eragon Credit Activation - Tony Cephas @ Thu 28 May 2026 4pm - 4:20pm (WAT) (cephastony40@gmail.com)

messageText:
One model name. The router picks per request based on task complexity. No routing fee.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

Picking the right model for every request is a job. openrouter/auto is the model name you use when you want OpenRouter to do that job for you. Pass it as the model on any request. The router classifies the request, picks a model from the auto pool based on task complexity, and runs it. You pay the standard rate for whichever model serves the response. There is no routing fee.

See the auto pool ( https://openrouter.ai/models/openrouter/auto?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a2_auto_routing&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A3+Auto+Router&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

response = client.chat.completions.create
(   model="openrouter/auto",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Summarize this document"}], )


print(response.model)  # which model actually served the request

 

A few things worth knowing:

A simple classification does not need a frontier model. A nuanced analysis does not belong on a small one. The router handles the call without you writing any branching logic.

Roughly 63 percent of auto traffic routes to models priced under $1 per million tokens. That is how simple prompts stop hitting expensive models without any routing code on your side, and it is the reason most teams see lower per-request cost on auto than on a single pinned frontier model.

You can still pass route="fallback" alongside auto if you want a named backstop on top of automatic selection. If the auto pick fails, the fallback list runs in order until one returns a response.

The auto pool changes as new models launch. You do not have to update your code when it does. Read response.model in the response if you want to see what the router picked on any given call, which is useful for logging and for spotting cost drift.

When not to use it. If you already know the exact model you want for a given call, pin the model string directly. Auto is for the 80 percent of traffic where the choice does not matter, not the 20 percent where it does. Most apps end up using both.

The next email covers what changes when you add credits to your account.

messageText:
Eragon Credit Activation - Tony Cephas
Thursday May 28, 2026 ⋅ 8am – 8:20am
Pacific Time - Los Angeles



Quick 20-minute call to walk through your $10K Eragon credit activation and  
get everything set up.

Organizer
Josh Sirota
josh@eragon.ai

Guests
Josh Sirota - organizer
cephastony40@gmail.com
lance@eragon.ai
View all guest info  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=YW5idXRhc3FvYWluNmszcjA2ZG5lNGJyNjggbGFuY2VAZXJhZ29uLmFp&tok=MTQjam9zaEBlcmFnb24uYWliMDIwMjNmZmRkNTQ2MzRjZGRjZGI3OGU1ZmFmZWRlZmU5Mjg0ZTA4&ctz=America%2FLos_Angeles&hl=en&es=1

Reply for lance@eragon.ai and view more details  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?action=VIEW&eid=YW5idXRhc3FvYWluNmszcjA2ZG5lNGJyNjggbGFuY2VAZXJhZ29uLmFp&tok=MTQjam9zaEBlcmFnb24uYWliMDIwMjNmZmRkNTQ2MzRjZGRjZGI3OGU1ZmFmZWRlZmU5Mjg0ZTA4&ctz=America%2FLos_Angeles&hl=en&es=1
Your attendance is optional.

~~//~~
Invitation from Google Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/calendar/

You are receiving this email because you are subscribed to calendar  
notifications. To stop receiving these emails, go to  
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/r/settings, select this calendar, and  
change "Other notifications".

Forwarding this invitation could allow any recipient to send a response to  
the organizer, be added to the guest list, invite others regardless of  
their own invitation status, or modify your RSVP.

Learn more https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/37135#forwarding

messageText:
+ Graham
+ Orr

Graham, Orr,

Meet Rishabh and Lance from the tech team at Eragon (Rishabh is by the way
co-founder and CTO).

Can you share some mid to complex prompt you or your team are doing on a
daily basis with Claude Co-work?
We would like to evaluate how eragon can perform the same level of quality
output vs Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini /… while showing a lower cost of
token consumption and ultimately reduce $ spent with Frontier model.

Thanks,


Olivier Elbaz
Global Head of Expansion
m: +972 58 780 55 76
www.letsdeel.com


Le jeu. 28 mai 2026 à 00:03, Rishabh Tiwari <rishabh@eragon.ai> a écrit :

> "First email from (rishabh@eragon.ai). Be cautious
> Hi Olivier,
>
> Quick follow up on the sample workflows, Lance is amazing, he has already
> prepared the eval pipeline that we discussed yesterday, we are now just
> waiting to run it on workflows that are most relevant to Deel. Pls send the
> sample workflows at your earliest convenience.
>
> Thanks and regards
> Rishabh
>
> On Tue, May 26, 2026 at 3:11 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Hi Olivier,
>>
>> We’re currently doing some testing on the Eragon routing and we’d love to
>> do some testing to measure the improvement that our routing system provides
>> on the type of work that you guys do.
>>
>> Can you please send us 4-5 example workflows that your team at Deel use
>> regularly?
>>
>> Thanks!
>> Lance
>>
>

messageText:
Hi Olivier,

Quick follow up on the sample workflows, Lance is amazing, he has already
prepared the eval pipeline that we discussed yesterday, we are now just
waiting to run it on workflows that are most relevant to Deel. Pls send the
sample workflows at your earliest convenience.

Thanks and regards
Rishabh

On Tue, May 26, 2026 at 3:11 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Olivier,
>
> We’re currently doing some testing on the Eragon routing and we’d love to
> do some testing to measure the improvement that our routing system provides
> on the type of work that you guys do.
>
> Can you please send us 4-5 example workflows that your team at Deel use
> regularly?
>
> Thanks!
> Lance
>

messageText:
From May 30, new projects won't expose tables to the Data API by default.  

Hi there,

On May 30, 2026, new Supabase projects won't expose tables in the "public" schema to the Data API by default. Any new table you create in "public" will require an explicit GRANT before it can be accessed through PostgREST, GraphQL, or supabase-js.

 

Existing projects keep the current default behavior until October 30, 2026.

 

What to do

 

Add explicit grants to your table-creation flow. The changelog post has the full SQL and migration guidance.

Read the changelog post ( https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/45329 )

Rollout dates

 

May 30, 2026: Default for all new projects
October 30, 2026: Enforced on new tables across all existing projects

Ahead of October 30, use the Security Advisor in your dashboard to review which tables are currently exposed to the Data API.

If you have any questions, please reach out to our support team ( https://supabase.com/dashboard/support/new?subject=Breaking%20Change%20-%20Data%20API%20Exposure ).

 

The team at Supabase

X ( https://x.com/supabase )
Custom ( https://github.com/supabase )Custom ( https://discord.supabase.com/ )Custom ( https://youtube.com/c/supabase )©2026 Supabase Inc.
3500 S. DuPont Highway, Kent 19901, Dover, Delaware, USA

The email was sent to lance@eragon.ai. To no longer receive these emails, unsubscribe ( ***REDACTED*** ) here.

messageText:
Pass a list of models. OpenRouter retries the next one if your primary fails. No app changes.  

OpenRouter
OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Hi Lance,

Provider outages and rate limits are the reason most LLM apps end up with a queue, a retry layer, and an oncall rotation. They do not have to be.

OpenRouter accepts a list of models on every request. If your first choice fails or rate-limits, the request runs against the next one in the list automatically. Same response shape, same SDK, no changes to your app code.

Read the routing docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/provider-routing?utm_source=email&utm_medium=lifecycle&utm_campaign=onboarding_v2&utm_content=a1_feature_expansion&utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

client.chat.completions.create
(   model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6",
  extra_body={
    "models": [
      "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6",
      "openai/gpt-5.4",
      "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview"
    ],
    "route": "fallback"
  },
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "..."}] )


 

A few things worth knowing:

• The list order is the priority order. OpenRouter walks it top to bottom.
• There is no extra fee for fallback routing. You pay the price of whichever model actually serves the request.
• Rate limits, 5xx errors, content refusals, and context-length errors all count as failures. The next model picks up automatically.
• You can mix providers in the same chain. Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google together is fine.

The next email covers openrouter/auto, which picks the model for you based on the request shape. Useful when you do not want to maintain your own list.

openrouter.ai ( https://openrouter.ai?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  X ( https://x.com/openrouter?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )  ·  Docs ( https://openrouter.ai/docs?utm_campaign=Onboarding+Flow+-+v4+April+2026&utm_content=A2+Fallback+Routing&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Griffin

*Subject:* Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2

Hi Kenny,

Thank you for your time today! It was great to meet you and learn more
about Eragon AI. As discussed, here is the link to the proposal that
includes SOC 2 Type 2 with the option to include Type 1.

Our Proposal
<https://linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=http%3a%2f%2fwww.prescientaudit.com%2fproposal%2fclient_view%2fEwQ6tscNUnGMnHD3dbHJtSmWVR5MSGLIWtgNFKHoiYQRFPNv61IhIAcTmHCGaGaKo3x2A0fwwFfKuzS3zBuhn7IJqvUJaKfQ803RCkhiQYJgWIWxtaNIBNhCCVchYgBr&c=E,1,JqhbxQ0w30SXtV0oRfop1l5T8S7WsEeXQbgOrMuMdXEUtHbTG8qMeE_DVqNBq14KdXwoj_TKY9hnxCkgeJghO-1VGhq0JjvwDBTVtDipHWvGS2o,&typo=1>


   - *Next steps:* Click “Accept Proposal” to complete our audit intake
   form. This allows us to send
   you a formal Letter of Engagement for review & signature.


*How Are We Different?*

   - Global cybersecurity company and multi-framework accredited audit
   firm, certifying and attesting to 25+ frameworks (SOC, ISO, GDPR, HIPAA,
   HITRUST, PCI, FedRAMP, and more).
   - Recognized by top regulatory bodies, including IAS/UKAS, PCAOB, and
   FedRAMP.
   - Certified as a CREST, CIS 18, and CSA Star Top 20 organization.
   - Extensive expertise across Big 4, Big Tech, Financial, and Healthcare
   industries.
   - One of only 10 approved security assessors for AWS Startup Compliance
   Accelerator, Google Play Store, Meta CASA, and Microsoft SSPA programs,
   with qualified cloud security specialists on the audit team.
   - Seamless integration with GRC Compliance Management Tools for
   efficient evidence collection and data handling.
   - Risk-based, flexible audits focused on relevant controls and
   minimizing unnecessary exceptions.
   - Dedicated kickoff calls with Senior Auditors and CSMs to set clear
   expectations.
   - Proactive support with feedback sessions, evidence review, and early
   mitigation of findings to avoid surprises during audits.
   - Slack channel for seamless and direct communication.


   - Real security solutions beyond certifications, including cloud
   security implementation, continuous vulnerability testing, incident
   response, and post-breach remediation for clients like Citigroup, FIS, and
   BCBS.



Let me know if you have any questions.


Best,

Griffin
