subject: Fwd: My calendly - for our call whenever!

messageText:
Hey Sol and thanks Irene.

Adding Lance and Rishabh on the team.

Can you find time with Sol this week?

On Tue, May 26, 2026 at 10:35 AM Sol Kim <solkim@college.harvard.edu> wrote:

> Hey Josh! Just bumping on this in case it slipped by-- super interested in
> eragon, would love to hear more!
>
> Sol
>
> ᐧ
>
> On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 2:15 PM Sol Kim <solkim@college.harvard.edu>
> wrote:
>
>> Thanks, Irene (bcc)! Great to meet you Josh, do you have time this week
>> for a quick chat? Super interested to hear what you guys are building.
>>
>> Sol
>>
>> ᐧ
>>
>> On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 1:37 PM Irene Koo <irene@somacap.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi both!
>>>
>>> You have context, but quick primer -
>>>
>>> Sol <https://www.linkedin.com/in/solkim0/> is on leave from Harvard,
>>> currently doing research at Exa. Josh
>>> <https://www.linkedin.com/in/josh-sirota/> is the founder of Eragon
>>> <https://www.eragon.ai>, building AI Operating Systems.
>>>
>>> Sol is interested in research-first roles in high-talent density
>>> environments - thought you two would have a good chat!
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Irene
>>>
>>> --
>>> Irene Koo
>>> 949-228-7892
>>>
>>

messageText:
Hey Lance, I’m still waiting on the final share count, but wanted to send
over the current version so you can take a look.

Please let me know if I missed anything. If everything else looks good,
I’ll add the share count once I have it and then we can sign.

Best,
Dave

On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 11:45 AM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Dave,
>
> Can you please tell me the post-termination exercise period for the stock
> options?
>
> Thanks,
> Lance
>
> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 5:24 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> That is correct, thank you Dave.
>>
>> Best,
>> Lance
>>
>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 5:17 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Lance,
>>>
>>> My understanding is that you’re not ready to sign yet and want to
>>> clarify a few things first:
>>>
>>>    - You feel the scope of the role is closer to founding engineer,
>>>    given how early you’re joining and the level of responsibility.
>>>    - You feel the comp should reflect that and suggest $300K base and
>>>    2% equity.
>>>    - You want the offer letter to include both the approximate
>>>    ownership percentage and the current number of outstanding shares.
>>>    - You want clarity on what happens if the company is acquired,
>>>    including whether there is a double trigger acceleration clause.
>>>    - While this gets sorted out, you're asking to increase your current
>>>    pay to $170K annualized, rather than the $2K/week current
>>>    arrangement.
>>>
>>> Is this correct? Let me know if I missed anything.
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Dave
>>>
>>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 3:04 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>
>>>> I think I want to talk with Josh about the offer when he gets back. In
>>>> the meantime, can I temporarily be put on a contract with a prorated weekly
>>>> salary of $170,000 until I either sign full-time or decline the offer?
>>>> Thanks!
>>>>
>>>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 11:00 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Sure, no problem.
>>>>>
>>>>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 10:58 AM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Can I get the offer expiration extended by 3 weeks? There are some
>>>>>> things I’d like to discuss in person with either you or Josh. Thanks!
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Tue, May 5, 2026 at 1:33 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Lance - Spoke to Josh. He does not want to share the Plan. Happy
>>>>>>> to talk through any specific questions you have.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Dave
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> On Tue, May 5, 2026 at 12:33 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Can you send me the grant plan too please?
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 11:08 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Ok, no problem.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 10:26 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> busy fixing code, I'll have to push it back 1 day
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 2:04 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Sounds good
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 2:03 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks. I’ll read it over carefully tonight and get back to you
>>>>>>>>>>>> tomorrow.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 11:27 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>> Hey Lance,
>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>> I updated the offer letter to reflect a 5/4 start date and the
>>>>>>>>>>>>> vesting schedule you discussed with Josh. Please let me know if anything
>>>>>>>>>>>>> else needs to be changed or if you have any questions.
>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>>>>>>> Dave
>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 10:25 AM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks Dave!
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> The start date should be today, 5/4.
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> And according to Josh and my agreement, I would get 1% equity
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> in the company, vesting over 24 months with an 8-month cliff.
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks,
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Lance
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 9:38 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Lance,
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Please find your offer letter attached. I left the start
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> date blank for now, so let me know what start date you would prefer.
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Please let me know if you have any questions.
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> Dave
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>>>

messageText:
Hey! I did.  All is good. Can you send over executable?

On Mon, May 25, 2026 at 11:31 AM Guilherme Teodoro Ferrigatto <
guilherme.ferrigatto@deel.com> wrote:

> Hey Josh,
> Following up on my previous email.
> Have you had a chance to review it?
>
> *Thanks.*
>
>
> On Thu, May 21, 2026 at 10:03 AM Guilherme Teodoro Ferrigatto <
> guilherme.ferrigatto@deel.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Josh!
>> Sending the redlined DPA from our Data Privacy team, which includes the
>> AI clauses they added.
>>
>> *"I have added the AI clauses in the DPA and also left a comment in the
>> sub-processor section as you’ve mentioned Anthropic as a sub-processor
>> below but they did not include them in the DPA." *
>>
>> Please have you or your team review it and let us know if we are good to
>> proceed.
>>
>> Thank you!
>> Guilherme Ferrigatto | LinkedIn
>> <https://hsassets.deel.com/hubfs/Deel/Logos/email-signature-2026.html#>
>> Senior Procurement Manager
>> ()
>>
>> [image: Deel]
>>
>>
>>
>> On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 5:39 PM <josh@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Guilherme,
>>>
>>> Happy to answer — yes, Eragon requires user login (web-based, no local
>>> installation required).
>>>
>>> On the integrations:
>>>
>>> - SAML: Yes, supported. We can configure SAML-based SSO with Deel's
>>> identity provider (e.g. Okta) for seamless single sign-on.
>>> - SCIM: Yes, supported. We support SCIM 2.0 for automated user
>>> provisioning and deprovisioning via your IdP.
>>>
>>> Happy to loop in our technical team to coordinate the SSO and SCIM
>>> setup with your IT team directly.
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Josh
>>>
>>

messageText:
Hello,

We wanted to alert you that your account balance has crossed the $16000.00 threshold you set as the total monthly API spend for the Dream Flow workspace in your organization “Eragon.” Per your configuration, further API usage is now paused for this workspace until midnight UTC on June 1, 2026.

If you'd like to modify your alert settings at any time, you can visit your Alert Settings page in your account dashboard. There, you can remove this limit to immediately re-enable API access for this workspace. Otherwise, API access will be re-enabled at the start of next month.

Warmly,
The Anthropic Team

For any further questions, please visit our help center.
Claude.ai · Research · Products · Company

messageText:
Security incident: rotate your Composio API keys before 11:00 PM PST, May 23 — we'll delete older keys after that. Follow the bulletin for the latest.

Hi team,

We're writing with an update on the recent security incident at Composio. The latest details are always on our Security Bulletin https://composio.dev/blog/composio-may-2026-security-incident.

Our ongoing forensic investigation suggests that some Composio API keys may have been leaked. We don't yet know the full scope, but as a precaution, we're asking all customers to:

 * Rotate your Composio API keys. Create a new key in the dashboard https://dashboard.composio.dev/ and switch your apps over to it before 11:00 PM PST, May 23 (6:00 AM UTC, May 24). After that cutoff, we will delete older keys created before 11:59 PM PST, May 22, so rotating beforehand avoids disruption.

 * Enable IP allowlisting. We have rolled it out in the dashboard - we recommend configuring allowlisted IPs https://dashboard.composio.dev/~/settings/ip-allowlist for your keys as an added layer of protection.

 * Revoke connected accounts. We recommend asking your users to revoke their connected-account tokens and API keys and reconnect. We've revoked every token we could from our end, but some we're not able to revoke ourselves - see the bulletin for details.

Separately, we already revoked 5,241 API keys, which were part of an auxiliary cache service, the attacker likely had access to during the breach window. Those keys had a higher potential of being compromised, so as a preventative measure we've already revoked them - this may have caused some downtime for production customers, and rotating to a new key resolves it.

We'll keep posting updates to the Security Bulletin https://composio.dev/blog/composio-may-2026-security-incident as the investigation continues - please keep following it. If you have questions or need help rotating, just reply to this email.

- Security Team, Composio

Composio Inc. · 2 Bryant St, San Francisco, CA 94105
composio.dev https://composio.dev · Unsubscribe ***REDACTED***

messageText:
Thanks, Lance. Moving you to bcc.

Griffin - nice to meet you. I'm excited to get this kicked off. Let me know
what you need from me in order to proceed.

Best,
Dave

On Thu, May 21, 2026 at 1:39 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Griffin,
>
> This is Dave, our COO at Eragon. He will take over the SOC2 process.
>
> Thanks,
> Lance
>
> On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 5:30 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Hi Griffin and Kenny,
>>
>> Thanks for the handoff.
>> I think we have everything we need for now, thanks. We will review your
>> proposal and follow up with you by next Tuesday.
>>
>> Best,
>> Lance
>>
>>
>> On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 5:21 PM Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hey this is lances contact
>>>
>>> On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 6:21 AM Griffin Mello <
>>> griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>
>>>> That sounds good!
>>>>
>>>> Would you mind making the intro to Lance so we can get the next steps
>>>> in place?
>>>>
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Griffin
>>>> ------------------------------
>>>> *From:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Sent:* Tuesday, May 19, 2026 7:54 PM
>>>> *To:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
>>>>
>>>> *Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>>>>
>>>> Hey Griffin,
>>>>
>>>> Lance will be the new POC for our SOC2.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Kenny
>>>>
>>>
>>>> On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 10:18 AM Griffin Mello <
>>>> griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>
>>>> Have you had an opportunity to review the proposal?
>>>>
>>>> Happy to set up another call to answer any questions you may have.
>>>>
>>>> Let me know!
>>>>
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Griffin
>>>> ------------------------------
>>>> *From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
>>>> *Sent:* Tuesday, May 12, 2026 12:24 PM
>>>> *To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
>>>> josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>>>>
>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>
>>>> Hope you had a great weekend!
>>>>
>>>> I wanted to follow up and see if there’s anything else you need from my
>>>> side to keep things moving forward. Happy to answer any questions or
>>>> coordinate next steps as needed.
>>>>
>>>> Please let me know when you have a chance.
>>>>
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Griffin
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> ------------------------------
>>>> *From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
>>>> *Sent:* Friday, May 8, 2026 9:47 AM
>>>> *To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
>>>> josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>>>>
>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>
>>>> Happy Friday! Do you have any questions or feedback about the proposal?
>>>>
>>>> Let me know what else you need from my side to move forward.
>>>>
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Griffin
>>>> ------------------------------
>>>> *From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
>>>> *Sent:* Wednesday, May 6, 2026 3:45 PM
>>>> *To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
>>>> josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
>>>> *Subject:* Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>>>>
>>>> Hi Kenny,
>>>>
>>>> Thank you for your time today! It was great to meet you and learn more
>>>> about Eragon AI. As discussed, here is the link to the proposal that
>>>> includes SOC 2 Type 2 with the option to include Type 1.
>>>>
>>>> Our Proposal
>>>> <https://linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=http%3a%2f%2fwww.prescientaudit.com%2fproposal%2fclient_view%2fEwQ6tscNUnGMnHD3dbHJtSmWVR5MSGLIWtgNFKHoiYQRFPNv61IhIAcTmHCGaGaKo3x2A0fwwFfKuzS3zBuhn7IJqvUJaKfQ803RCkhiQYJgWIWxtaNIBNhCCVchYgBr&c=E,1,JqhbxQ0w30SXtV0oRfop1l5T8S7WsEeXQbgOrMuMdXEUtHbTG8qMeE_DVqNBq14KdXwoj_TKY9hnxCkgeJghO-1VGhq0JjvwDBTVtDipHWvGS2o,&typo=1>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>    - *Next steps:* Click “Accept Proposal” to complete our audit
>>>>    intake form. This allows us to send
>>>>    you a formal Letter of Engagement for review & signature.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *How Are We Different?*
>>>>
>>>>    - Global cybersecurity company and multi-framework accredited audit
>>>>    firm, certifying and attesting to 25+ frameworks (SOC, ISO, GDPR, HIPAA,
>>>>    HITRUST, PCI, FedRAMP, and more).
>>>>    - Recognized by top regulatory bodies, including IAS/UKAS, PCAOB,
>>>>    and FedRAMP.
>>>>    - Certified as a CREST, CIS 18, and CSA Star Top 20 organization.
>>>>    - Extensive expertise across Big 4, Big Tech, Financial, and
>>>>    Healthcare industries.
>>>>    - One of only 10 approved security assessors for AWS Startup
>>>>    Compliance Accelerator, Google Play Store, Meta CASA, and Microsoft SSPA
>>>>    programs, with qualified cloud security specialists on the audit team.
>>>>    - Seamless integration with GRC Compliance Management Tools for
>>>>    efficient evidence collection and data handling.
>>>>    - Risk-based, flexible audits focused on relevant controls and
>>>>    minimizing unnecessary exceptions.
>>>>    - Dedicated kickoff calls with Senior Auditors and CSMs to set
>>>>    clear expectations.
>>>>    - Proactive support with feedback sessions, evidence review, and
>>>>    early mitigation of findings to avoid surprises during audits.
>>>>    - Slack channel for seamless and direct communication.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>    - Real security solutions beyond certifications, including cloud
>>>>    security implementation, continuous vulnerability testing, incident
>>>>    response, and post-breach remediation for clients like Citigroup, FIS, and
>>>>    BCBS.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Let me know if you have any questions.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>>
>>>> Griffin
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *Confidentiality Notice*

>>>>

messageText:
Hi Josh!
Sending the redlined DPA from our Data Privacy team, which includes the AI
clauses they added.

*"I have added the AI clauses in the DPA and also left a comment in the
sub-processor section as you’ve mentioned Anthropic as a sub-processor
below but they did not include them in the DPA." *

Please have you or your team review it and let us know if we are good to
proceed.

Thank you!
Guilherme Ferrigatto | LinkedIn
<https://hsassets.deel.com/hubfs/Deel/Logos/email-signature-2026.html#>
Senior Procurement Manager
()

[image: Deel]



On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 5:39 PM <josh@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Guilherme,
>
> Happy to answer — yes, Eragon requires user login (web-based, no local
> installation required).
>
> On the integrations:
>
> - SAML: Yes, supported. We can configure SAML-based SSO with Deel's
> identity provider (e.g. Okta) for seamless single sign-on.
> - SCIM: Yes, supported. We support SCIM 2.0 for automated user
> provisioning and deprovisioning via your IdP.
>
> Happy to loop in our technical team to coordinate the SSO and SCIM
> setup with your IT team directly.
>
> Best,
> Josh
>

messageText:
Hey this is lances contact

On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 6:21 AM Griffin Mello <
griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com> wrote:

> Hi Kenny,
>
> That sounds good!
>
> Would you mind making the intro to Lance so we can get the next steps in
> place?
>
> Thanks,
> Griffin
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
> *Sent:* Tuesday, May 19, 2026 7:54 PM
> *To:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
>
> *Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>
> Hey Griffin,
>
> Lance will be the new POC for our SOC2.
>
> Best,
> Kenny
>
> On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 10:18 AM Griffin Mello <
> griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com> wrote:
>
> Hi Kenny,
>
> Have you had an opportunity to review the proposal?
>
> Happy to set up another call to answer any questions you may have.
>
> Let me know!
>
> Thanks,
> Griffin
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
> *Sent:* Tuesday, May 12, 2026 12:24 PM
> *To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
> *Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
> josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
> *Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>
> Hi Kenny,
>
> Hope you had a great weekend!
>
> I wanted to follow up and see if there’s anything else you need from my
> side to keep things moving forward. Happy to answer any questions or
> coordinate next steps as needed.
>
> Please let me know when you have a chance.
>
> Thanks,
> Griffin
>
>
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
> *Sent:* Friday, May 8, 2026 9:47 AM
> *To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
> *Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
> josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
> *Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>
> Hi Kenny,
>
> Happy Friday! Do you have any questions or feedback about the proposal?
>
> Let me know what else you need from my side to move forward.
>
> Thanks,
> Griffin
> ------------------------------
> *From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
> *Sent:* Wednesday, May 6, 2026 3:45 PM
> *To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
> *Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
> josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
> *Subject:* Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2
>
> Hi Kenny,
>
> Thank you for your time today! It was great to meet you and learn more
> about Eragon AI. As discussed, here is the link to the proposal that
> includes SOC 2 Type 2 with the option to include Type 1.
>
> Our Proposal
> <https://linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=http%3a%2f%2fwww.prescientaudit.com%2fproposal%2fclient_view%2fEwQ6tscNUnGMnHD3dbHJtSmWVR5MSGLIWtgNFKHoiYQRFPNv61IhIAcTmHCGaGaKo3x2A0fwwFfKuzS3zBuhn7IJqvUJaKfQ803RCkhiQYJgWIWxtaNIBNhCCVchYgBr&c=E,1,JqhbxQ0w30SXtV0oRfop1l5T8S7WsEeXQbgOrMuMdXEUtHbTG8qMeE_DVqNBq14KdXwoj_TKY9hnxCkgeJghO-1VGhq0JjvwDBTVtDipHWvGS2o,&typo=1>
>
>
>    - *Next steps:* Click “Accept Proposal” to complete our audit intake
>    form. This allows us to send
>    you a formal Letter of Engagement for review & signature.
>
>
> *How Are We Different?*
>
>    - Global cybersecurity company and multi-framework accredited audit
>    firm, certifying and attesting to 25+ frameworks (SOC, ISO, GDPR, HIPAA,
>    HITRUST, PCI, FedRAMP, and more).
>    - Recognized by top regulatory bodies, including IAS/UKAS, PCAOB, and
>    FedRAMP.
>    - Certified as a CREST, CIS 18, and CSA Star Top 20 organization.
>    - Extensive expertise across Big 4, Big Tech, Financial, and
>    Healthcare industries.
>    - One of only 10 approved security assessors for AWS Startup
>    Compliance Accelerator, Google Play Store, Meta CASA, and Microsoft SSPA
>    programs, with qualified cloud security specialists on the audit team.
>    - Seamless integration with GRC Compliance Management Tools for
>    efficient evidence collection and data handling.
>    - Risk-based, flexible audits focused on relevant controls and
>    minimizing unnecessary exceptions.
>    - Dedicated kickoff calls with Senior Auditors and CSMs to set clear
>    expectations.
>    - Proactive support with feedback sessions, evidence review, and early
>    mitigation of findings to avoid surprises during audits.
>    - Slack channel for seamless and direct communication.
>
>
>    - Real security solutions beyond certifications, including cloud
>    security implementation, continuous vulnerability testing, incident
>    response, and post-breach remediation for clients like Citigroup, FIS, and
>    BCBS.
>
>
>
> Let me know if you have any questions.
>
>
> Best,
>
> Griffin
>
>
>
> *Confidentiality Notice*
> The content of this communication, along with any attachments, is covered
> by federal and state law governing electronic communications and may
> contain confidential and legally privileged information. If the reader of
> this message is not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that
> any dissemination, distribution, use or copying of the information
> contained herein is strictly prohibited. If you have received this
> communication in error, please immediately contact us by email at
> sec.team@prescientsecurity.com. Thank You.
>
>
> *Confidentiality Notice*
> The content of this communication, along with any attachments, is covered

> sec.team@prescientsecurity.com. Thank You.
>

preview.subject: Confirm Your Signup

subject: Confirm Your Signup

messageText:
Hi Guilherme,

Happy to answer — yes, Eragon requires user login (web-based, no local
installation required).

On the integrations:

- SAML: Yes, supported. We can configure SAML-based SSO with Deel's
identity provider (e.g. Okta) for seamless single sign-on.
- SCIM: Yes, supported. We support SCIM 2.0 for automated user
provisioning and deprovisioning via your IdP.

Happy to loop in our technical team to coordinate the SSO and SCIM
setup with your IT team directly.

Best,
Josh

From: "The Google Workspace Team" [workspace-noreply@google.com]
Subject:  You have a new Google account for eragon.ai

Welcome to your new account

## Welcome to your new Google Account for eragon.ai

Hello,

You have a new Google Account with the eragon.ai organization.

Sign in to your Google Account to access the Google services your  
organization provides. If your organization has Google Workspace, Google  
services may include business-grade versions of Google Drive, Gmail, and  
other Google services you can use to collaborate with your team.

**Password:** Click **Sign in** below to set your password and sign in. To  
keep your account secure, follow these password guidelines  
(https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/7521867?utm_source=9017515&utm_medium=email;).

Sign in  
(https://accounts.google.com/RP?c=CMrpjdzFltrG9gEQnN6wl5Lvp-2YAQ&uc=ac&hl=en&continue=https://workspace.google.com/dashboard&fc=1&flowName=GlifWebSignIn)

For your security, the reset password link expires after 48 hours. After  
that, please contact your administrator  
(https://support.google.com/a/answer/6208960?utm_source=9017515&utm_medium=email;)  
for your password.

Regards,

The Google Workspace Team


(C) 2026  Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043 *   
Google Cloud EMEA Ltd., 70 Sir John Rogerson’s Quay, Dublin 2, Ireland.

You're receiving this mandatory email service announcement to update you  
about important changes to your Google Workspace product or account.

messageText:
Hi Josh!
Thanks for the quick turnaround. I have shared your responses internally.
In addition, our IT team asked a couple of questions.
Please see below.

*Could you please confirm if the Eragon, product or service, encompassing
their SaaS platform, website, tool, or technology, requires user login or
installation on local machines? **If the answer is yes, could you inquire
whether they support the following integrations:*

*SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) **SCIM (System for Cross-domain
Identity Management)*

Thank you!
Guilherme Ferrigatto | LinkedIn
<https://hsassets.deel.com/hubfs/Deel/Logos/email-signature-2026.html#>
Senior Procurement Manager
()

[image: Deel]



On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 4:07 PM <josh@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Guilherme,
>
> Thanks for the thorough questions - happy to work through these with
> your Data Compliance team. Please find our responses below.
>
> ---
>
> PRIVACY ASSESSMENT
>
> 1. Nature and purpose of processing personal data
> Eragon is an AI operating system deployed to power AI agents and
> assistants within your organization. In providing this service, Eragon
> processes personal data solely to fulfill the functionality of the AI
> agent - i.e., to understand and respond to queries, automate tasks,
> and integrate with connected tools (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Calendar) on
> behalf of authorized users. Processing is always at the direction of
> the end user or administrator and is limited to what is necessary to
> perform the requested function.
>
> 2. Categories of data subjects
> Based on Deel's intended deployment, the relevant data subjects would
> be: Deel employees and contractors who are provisioned as users on the
> Eragon platform. No EOR employee or client employee data is expected
> to be in scope unless explicitly routed to the system by a Deel
> administrator.
>
> 3. Complete list of personal data categories
> The following categories may be processed depending on the integrations
> enabled:
> - Name and email address (user identity, authentication)
> - Work communications content (emails, messages, calendar events -
> only where the user explicitly connects those integrations)
> - Job title / organizational role (from connected directories)
> - Usage/interaction logs (queries sent to the assistant, responses
> received)
>
> Payment information is not processed by Eragon.
>
> 4. Security measures and certifications
> Eragon has completed all SOC 2 Type II controls and is currently
> undergoing formal audit through Vanta. All technical and
> organizational controls are fully implemented and passing - we are in
> the final audit and review stage. We are happy to share our Vanta
> trust report and control evidence upon request. Our security posture
> includes:
> - Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
> - MFA enforced across all infrastructure (AWS IAM, Google Workspace,
> GitHub)
> - Role-based access controls with least-privilege principles
> - Incident response plan, access control policies, and data management
> policies in place
> - Infrastructure hosted on AWS with redundant S3 backups across regions
>
> 5. Sub-processor list
> Our key sub-processors are:
>
>   Amazon Web Services (AWS) - Cloud infrastructure, compute, storage -
> United States
>   Anthropic - AI inference (language model processing) - United States
>
> We can provide a full sub-processor addendum upon request.
>
> 6. Where is personal data stored?
> All personal data is stored in the United States on AWS infrastructure.
>
> ---
>
> AI VENDOR QUESTIONS
>
> 1. Purposes of AI systems / outputs and decisions
> Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude models to power AI agent capabilities.
> Specific outputs include: natural language responses to user queries,
> automated task execution (e.g. drafting emails, scheduling,
> summarizing documents), and workflow automation via connected
> integrations. No automated decisions with legal or similarly
> significant effects are made by the system - all meaningful outputs
> require human review or action.
>
> 2. Transparency documentation
> We maintain internal documentation of our AI system architecture, data
> flow diagrams, and model provider agreements. We are happy to share
> these under NDA upon request. Anthropic's usage policies and
> transparency documentation are publicly available at
> anthropic.com/usage-policy.
>
> 3. AI policies and bias mitigation
> Eragon's AI policies include: prohibited use cases (no use of AI for
> discriminatory decisions, no generation of harmful content), model
> output review requirements, and human-in-the-loop requirements for
> consequential actions. Anthropic's Claude models have undergone
> extensive safety and bias evaluation - documentation is available via
> Anthropic's model card and responsible scaling policy.
>
> 4. Does Eragon use Deel's data to train AI systems?
> No. Eragon does not use customer data to train any AI models - our own
> or third parties'. Our agreement with Anthropic is an enterprise
> agreement that explicitly prohibits Anthropic from using API
> inputs/outputs for model training. Data processed through the API is
> not retained by Anthropic beyond the immediate inference request.
>
> 5. Human oversight measures
> All AI agent actions are initiated by and attributable to a specific
> human user. Consequential external actions (sending emails, making API
> calls to third-party services) require explicit user instruction.
> Administrators have full audit logs of all agent activity. Users can
> pause, review, or override any agent action at any time.
>
> 6. Data subject rights (access and deletion)
> Yes. Data subjects can request access to their interaction history and
> request deletion at any time. Requests can be submitted to
> privacy@eragon.ai. Upon verified request, all personal data associated
> with a user account will be deleted within 30 days, including
> interaction logs and any cached context.
>
> 7. Third-party AI systems / ENT agreements and DPAs
> Yes, Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude via the Anthropic API. We have an
> enterprise agreement with Anthropic that includes:
> - A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) prohibiting use of customer data
> for model training
> - No data retention beyond the immediate API request
> - US-based data processing
>
> We can provide a copy of our Anthropic DPA upon request.
>
> ---
>
> Please let us know if any answers require more detail or if you would
> like supporting documentation for any of the above. Looking forward to
> getting the onboarding completed.
>
> Best,
> Josh Sirota
> CEO, Eragon Corp
>

Hi Guilherme,

Thanks for the thorough questions - happy to work through these with
your Data Compliance team. Please find our responses below.

---

PRIVACY ASSESSMENT

1. Nature and purpose of processing personal data
Eragon is an AI operating system deployed to power AI agents and
assistants within your organization. In providing this service, Eragon
processes personal data solely to fulfill the functionality of the AI
agent - i.e., to understand and respond to queries, automate tasks,
and integrate with connected tools (e.g. Gmail, Slack, Calendar) on
behalf of authorized users. Processing is always at the direction of
the end user or administrator and is limited to what is necessary to
perform the requested function.

2. Categories of data subjects
Based on Deel's intended deployment, the relevant data subjects would
be: Deel employees and contractors who are provisioned as users on the
Eragon platform. No EOR employee or client employee data is expected
to be in scope unless explicitly routed to the system by a Deel
administrator.

3. Complete list of personal data categories
The following categories may be processed depending on the integrations enabled:
- Name and email address (user identity, authentication)
- Work communications content (emails, messages, calendar events -
only where the user explicitly connects those integrations)
- Job title / organizational role (from connected directories)

Payment information is not processed by Eragon.

4. Security measures and certifications
Eragon has completed all SOC 2 Type II controls and is currently
undergoing formal audit through Vanta. All technical and
organizational controls are fully implemented and passing - we are in
the final audit and review stage. We are happy to share our Vanta
trust report and control evidence upon request. Our security posture
includes:
- Encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+)
- MFA enforced across all infrastructure (AWS IAM, Google Workspace, GitHub)
- Role-based access controls with least-privilege principles
- Incident response plan, access control policies, and data management
policies in place
- Infrastructure hosted on AWS with redundant S3 backups across regions

5. Sub-processor list
Our key sub-processors are:

  Amazon Web Services (AWS) - Cloud infrastructure, compute, storage -
United States
  Anthropic - AI inference (language model processing) - United States

We can provide a full sub-processor addendum upon request.

6. Where is personal data stored?
All personal data is stored in the United States on AWS infrastructure.

---

AI VENDOR QUESTIONS

1. Purposes of AI systems / outputs and decisions
Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude models to power AI agent capabilities.
Specific outputs include: natural language responses to user queries,
automated task execution (e.g. drafting emails, scheduling,
summarizing documents), and workflow automation via connected
integrations. No automated decisions with legal or similarly
significant effects are made by the system - all meaningful outputs
require human review or action.

2. Transparency documentation
We maintain internal documentation of our AI system architecture, data
flow diagrams, and model provider agreements. We are happy to share
these under NDA upon request. Anthropic's usage policies and
transparency documentation are publicly available at
anthropic.com/usage-policy.

3. AI policies and bias mitigation
Eragon's AI policies include: prohibited use cases (no use of AI for
discriminatory decisions, no generation of harmful content), model
output review requirements, and human-in-the-loop requirements for
consequential actions. Anthropic's Claude models have undergone
extensive safety and bias evaluation - documentation is available via
Anthropic's model card and responsible scaling policy.

4. Does Eragon use Deel's data to train AI systems?
No. Eragon does not use customer data to train any AI models - our own
or third parties'. Our agreement with Anthropic is an enterprise
agreement that explicitly prohibits Anthropic from using API
inputs/outputs for model training. Data processed through the API is
not retained by Anthropic beyond the immediate inference request.

5. Human oversight measures
All AI agent actions are initiated by and attributable to a specific
human user. Consequential external actions (sending emails, making API
calls to third-party services) require explicit user instruction.
Administrators have full audit logs of all agent activity. Users can
pause, review, or override any agent action at any time.

6. Data subject rights (access and deletion)
Yes. Data subjects can request access to their interaction history and
request deletion at any time. Requests can be submitted to
privacy@eragon.ai. Upon verified request, all personal data associated
with a user account will be deleted within 30 days, including
interaction logs and any cached context.

7. Third-party AI systems / ENT agreements and DPAs
Yes, Eragon uses Anthropic's Claude via the Anthropic API. We have an
enterprise agreement with Anthropic that includes:
- A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) prohibiting use of customer data
for model training
- No data retention beyond the immediate API request
- US-based data processing

We can provide a copy of our Anthropic DPA upon request.

---

Please let us know if any answers require more detail or if you would
like supporting documentation for any of the above. Looking forward to
getting the onboarding completed.

Best,
Josh Sirota
CEO, Eragon Corp

messageText:
Thanks. Will revert back shortly.

On Wed, May 20, 2026 at 9:44 AM Guilherme Teodoro Ferrigatto <
guilherme.ferrigatto@deel.com> wrote:

> Thanks João for looping me in.
>
> Hey Eragon team,
> Nice to e-meet you all.
> In order to conclude the vendor onboarding process we have a few
> outstanding questions from our Data Compliance team. We would appreciate it
> if you could provide the answers.
> Please check below.
> Also, they are adding a few AI clauses to your DPA and we will share it
> soon for review.
>
> *Privacy Assessment*
>
>    1.
>
>    The nature and purpose of processing: please explain in detail why
>    this vendor processes or accesses personal data.
>    2.
>
>    Categories of data subjects e.g. Deel employees, client employees, EOR
>    employees, Contractors, etc. — I see the DPA appendix 1 is very broad but
>    we need to be more specific here about what kind of data could be captured.
>    3.
>
>    *Complete *list of the categories of personal data shared with the
>    vendor (e.g. name, contact details, payment info, etc.)
>    4.
>
>    Does the vendor have a list of security measures or hold any security
>    certifications (e.g. SOC2; ISO 27001)?
>    5.
>
>    Sub-processor list: does the vendor have a list of sub-processors they
>    can share with us?
>    6.
>
>    Where is personal data stored (country)?
>
> *AI Vendors: *
>
>    1.
>
>    For what purposes does the vendor use AI systems? Please explain in as
>    much detail as possible any outputs/decisions.
>    2.
>
>    Does the vendor have any transparency documents or documentation of
>    use - this helps us understand how they handle data and how decisions are
>    made.
>    3.
>
>    Please provide any AI policies and bias mitigation plans.
>    4.
>
>    Does the vendor use Deel's data to train AI systems (their own or
>    third parties’ systems)?
>    5.
>
>    What human oversight measures does this vendor have?
>    6.
>
>    Can data subjects access information about how their data is being
>    used and can they request deletion?
>    7.
>
>    Does the vendor use third party AI systems to support their tools? If
>    so, do they have ENT agreements and DPAs in place that prohibit data
>    retention and training?
>
>
> Thank you!
> Guilherme Ferrigatto | LinkedIn
> <https://hsassets.deel.com/hubfs/Deel/Logos/email-signature-2026.html#>
> Senior Procurement Manager
> ()

messageText:
Thanks João for looping me in.

Hey Eragon team,
Nice to e-meet you all.
In order to conclude the vendor onboarding process we have a few
outstanding questions from our Data Compliance team. We would appreciate it
if you could provide the answers.
Please check below.
Also, they are adding a few AI clauses to your DPA and we will share it
soon for review.

*Privacy Assessment*

   1.

   The nature and purpose of processing: please explain in detail why this
   vendor processes or accesses personal data.
   2.

   Categories of data subjects e.g. Deel employees, client employees, EOR
   employees, Contractors, etc. — I see the DPA appendix 1 is very broad but
   we need to be more specific here about what kind of data could be captured.
   3.

   *Complete *list of the categories of personal data shared with the
   vendor (e.g. name, contact details, payment info, etc.)
   4.

   Does the vendor have a list of security measures or hold any security
   certifications (e.g. SOC2; ISO 27001)?
   5.

   Sub-processor list: does the vendor have a list of sub-processors they
   can share with us?
   6.

   Where is personal data stored (country)?

*AI Vendors: *

   1.

   For what purposes does the vendor use AI systems? Please explain in as
   much detail as possible any outputs/decisions.
   2.

   Does the vendor have any transparency documents or documentation of use
   - this helps us understand how they handle data and how decisions are made.
   3.

   Please provide any AI policies and bias mitigation plans.
   4.

   Does the vendor use Deel's data to train AI systems (their own or third
   parties’ systems)?
   5.

   What human oversight measures does this vendor have?
   6.

   Can data subjects access information about how their data is being used
   and can they request deletion?
   7.

   Does the vendor use third party AI systems to support their tools? If
   so, do they have ENT agreements and DPAs in place that prohibit data
   retention and training?


Thank you!
Guilherme Ferrigatto | LinkedIn
<https://hsassets.deel.com/hubfs/Deel/Logos/email-signature-2026.html#>
Senior Procurement Manager
()

[image: Deel]

Hi Kenny,

Hope you had a great weekend!

I wanted to follow up and see if there’s anything else you need from my
side to keep things moving forward. Happy to answer any questions or
coordinate next steps as needed.

Please let me know when you have a chance.

Thanks,
Griffin


------------------------------
*From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
*Sent:* Friday, May 8, 2026 9:47 AM
*To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
*Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
*Subject:* Re: Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2

Hi Kenny,

Happy Friday! Do you have any questions or feedback about the proposal?

Let me know what else you need from my side to move forward.

Thanks,
Griffin
------------------------------
*From:* Griffin Mello <griffin.mello@prescientsecurity.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, May 6, 2026 3:45 PM
*To:* Kenny Xie <kenny@eragon.ai>
*Cc:* Prescient_Assurance <audit-support@prescientassurance.com>;
josh@eragon.ai <josh@eragon.ai>
*Subject:* Eragon AI <> Prescient Security - SOC 2 Type 2

Hi Kenny,

Thank you for your time today! It was great to meet you and learn more
about Eragon AI. As discussed, here is the link to the proposal that
includes SOC 2 Type 2 with the option to include Type 1.

Our Proposal
<http://www.prescientaudit.com/proposal/client_view/EwQ6tscNUnGMnHD3dbHJtSmWVR5MSGLIWtgNFKHoiYQRFPNv61IhIAcTmHCGaGaKo3x2A0fwwFfKuzS3zBuhn7IJqvUJaKfQ803RCkhiQYJgWIWxtaNIBNhCCVchYgBr>


   - *Next steps:* Click “Accept Proposal” to complete our audit intake
   form. This allows us to send
   you a formal Letter of Engagement for review & signature.


*How Are We Different?*

   - Global cybersecurity company and multi-framework accredited audit
   firm, certifying and attesting to 25+ frameworks (SOC, ISO, GDPR, HIPAA,
   HITRUST, PCI, FedRAMP, and more).
   - Recognized by top regulatory bodies, including IAS/UKAS, PCAOB, and
   FedRAMP.
   - Certified as a CREST, CIS 18, and CSA Star Top 20 organization.
   - Extensive expertise across Big 4, Big Tech, Financial, and Healthcare
   industries.
   - One of only 10 approved security assessors for AWS Startup Compliance
   Accelerator, Google Play Store, Meta CASA, and Microsoft SSPA programs,
   with qualified cloud security specialists on the audit team.
   - Seamless integration with GRC Compliance Management Tools for
   efficient evidence collection and data handling.
   - Risk-based, flexible audits focused on relevant controls and
   minimizing unnecessary exceptions.
   - Dedicated kickoff calls with Senior Auditors and CSMs to set clear
   expectations.
   - Proactive support with feedback sessions, evidence review, and early
   mitigation of findings to avoid surprises during audits.
   - Slack channel for seamless and direct communication.


   - Real security solutions beyond certifications, including cloud
   security implementation, continuous vulnerability testing, incident
   response, and post-breach remediation for clients like Citigroup, FIS, and
   BCBS.



Let me know if you have any questions.


Best,

Griffin



*Confidentiality Notice*
The content of this communication, along with any attachments, is covered
by federal and state law governing electronic communications and may
contain confidential and legally privileged information. If the reader of
this message is not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that
any dissemination, distribution, use or copying of the information
contained herein is strictly prohibited. If you have received this
communication in error, please immediately contact us by email at
sec.team@prescientsecurity.com. Thank You.

messageText:
Hi Josh,

I'm adding my colleague @Guilherme Teodoro Ferrigatto
<guilherme.ferrigatto@deel.com> since he is working on the vendor
onboarding process internally and could help you with the commercial
discussion in the near future.

Thank you!

*João Pinto*
Procurement Manager
*Calendar Link <https://calendar.app.google/F5jdbpdj52aaVU2J6>*
joao.pinto@deel.com



On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 5:07 PM Josh Sirota <josh@eragon.ai> wrote:

> EXT SENDER– Be cautious
> Hey Orr and Joao,
>
> Excited to get started. Adding Nick and Lance from my team.
>
> Let us know a couple of times that work for you tomorrow or Wednesday for
> 45-60 minutes.
>

messageText:
WELCOME TO BETTER AUTH!

Hi Lance Mathias,

Thanks for signing up! We're excited to have you on board.

If you haven't used Better Auth before, it's an open source authentication framework for TypeScript. Get started with our docs:

Get Started https://www.better-auth.com/docs/introduction


JOIN OUR COMMUNITY

We'd love to have you in our community! Join our Discord to connect with other developers, ask questions, and share what you're building.

Join our Discord https://discord.gg/better-auth   •   Star us on GitHub ⭐ https://github.com/better-auth/better-auth


NEED A DASHBOARD?

Once you've set up Better Auth, we offer a powerful dashboard with additional features to help you manage your authentication:

 * User Management — View, edit, and manage all your users
 * Analytics & Events — Track sign-ups, logins, and user activity
 * Audit Logs — Complete history of authentication events
 * Security Monitoring — Detect suspicious activity and threats
 * Email & SMS — Built-in transactional email and SMS support
 * Organizations — Multi-tenant support with team management
 * Branding — Customize your auth pages to match your brand
 * OAuth Clients — Manage your OAuth applications
 * Plugins — Extend functionality with our plugin system
 * Log Drains — Stream logs to your preferred destination

Explore the Dashboard https://dash.better-auth.com

----------------------------------------

Email sent by Better Auth.
If you didn't sign up for Better Auth, you can safely ignore this email.

subject: Reset Your Password

messageText:
It does, thanks for surfacing this, didn’t get this email.


On Wed, May 13, 2026 at 3:03 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Does this affect us?
>
> ---------- Forwarded message ---------
> From: Supabase <noreply@supabase.com>
> Date: Wed, May 13, 2026 at 11:10 AM
> Subject: Data API access changes May 30 for all new projects
> To: <lance@eragon.ai>
>
>
> New tables in the public schema will no longer be automatically exposed.
> Here's how to check if you’re affected, and what to do next.  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏
>  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏
>  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏
>  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­
> ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­
> ­ ­ ­ ­
>
> Hi there,
>
> Starting *May 30*, new Supabase projects will not expose tables in the
> "public" schema to the Data API by default. Any table you create in
> "public" after that date requires an explicit GRANT before supabase-js,
> PostgREST, or GraphQL can access it.
>
> *How to tell if you’re affected*
>
>
>
> We detected *2 project(s)* in your account that use the Data API.
>
>
>
> You are affected if:
>
>    - Your app uses the Data API (supabase-js, client libraries, or direct
>    HTTP to "/rest/v1/" or "/graphql/v1/")
>    - Your migrations or provisioning scripts create tables in "public"
>    without explicit grants
>
>
> You are not affected if:
>
>    - Your app connects to Postgres only via a direct connection string
>    (psql, ORMs, or an app server)
>
> Existing tables keep their current grants. This change applies to new
> tables in new projects from May 30, and all existing projects from October
> 30.
>
> *What to do*
>
>
>
> For new tables you want to expose via the Data API, make explicit grants
> part of your table-creation flow. Without an explicit GRANT, a role will
> not have access to the created table.
> -- Grant access per role
> grant select
>   on public.your_table
>   to anon;
>
> grant select, insert, update, delete
>   on public.your_table
>   to authenticated;
>
> grant select, insert, update, delete
>   on public.your_table
>   to service_role;
>
> -- Enable RLS
> alter table public.your_table
>   enable row level security;
>
> -- Add policies
> create policy "users can read their own rows"
>   on public.your_table
>   for select to authenticated
>   using (auth.uid() = user_id);
>
> If a grant is missing, PostgREST returns a "42501" error with the exact
> GRANT statement to fix it.
>
> *Rollout dates*
>
> *May 30, 2026:* Default for all new projects
> *October 30, 2026*: Enforced on all existing projects
>
> Learn more about this change
> <https://email.supabase.>
>
> Use the Security Advisor in your dashboard to review any existing tables.
>
>
> If you have any questions, please reach out via our support portal
> <https://email.supabase.>
> .
>
>
>
> The team at Supabase
> [image: X]
> <https://email.supabase.com/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnU0Z1QXNEQUx6eG9BUzc4YUFFQVo0aWlBb0hsdTlScUtfR3cxdXBrUT09IiwiaHJlZiI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5jb20vc3VwYWJhc2UiLCJpbnRlcm5hbCI6Ijg1YjgwYmE5MDI4Yjg4MDFiY2YxYTAwNCIsImxpbmtfaWQiOjEwNH0/0f55d9487a54fa4c85a5ff2a79f4fdc24614abc3e060dcd0145221731045f747> [image:

> <https://email.supabase.com/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnU0Z1QXNEQUx6eG9BUzc4YUFFQVo0aWlBb0hsdTlScUtfR3cxdXBrUT09IiwiaHJlZiI6Imh0dHBzOi8veW91dHViZS5jb20vYy9zdXBhYmFzZSIsImludGVybmFsIjoiODViODBiYTkwMjhiODgwMWJjZjFhMDA0IiwibGlua19pZCI6MTA3fQ/b038b1b850a1b9ff55cd6c4e49e7140db0949b8b2d50be84e57851774095bcac>

subject: Re: Data API access changes May 30 for all new projects

messageText:
Thank you, looking forward to it!

On Wed, May 13, 2026 at 11:34 AM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Great! I just sent you an invite for 11:00–11:30 a.m. on Friday.
>
> Looking forward to meeting you!
>
> Best,
> Lance
>
> On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 2:35 PM Quess Liu <quessliuqes@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Lance,
>> Thank you for reaching out.
>> I'm available between 11am-2pm PT Friday.
>> Kind regards
>> Quess Liu
>>
>> On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 2:25 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Quess,
>>>
>>> Nice to meet you! I would like to schedule a 30-minute meeting to learn
>>> more about your background.
>>>
>>> I am generally available on weekdays between 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM.
>>> Please let me know which time works best for you, and I will send you a
>>> calendar invitation with a meeting link.
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Lance
>>>
>>> On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 5:53 PM Quess Liu <quessliuqes@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Thank you, looking forward to it!
>>>>

>>>>>
>>>>> It was a pleasure speaking with you today. I’m looping in my colleague
>>>>> Lance so the two of you can find a time to connect.
>>>>>
>>>>> Best,
>>>>> Dave
>>>>>
>>>>

subject: Re: Quess // Lance

messageText:
Hi Lance,
Thank you for reaching out.
I'm available between 11am-2pm PT Friday.
Kind regards
Quess Liu

On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 2:25 PM Lance Mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Hi Quess,
>
> Nice to meet you! I would like to schedule a 30-minute meeting to learn
> more about your background.
>
> I am generally available on weekdays between 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM. Please
> let me know which time works best for you, and I will send you a calendar
> invitation with a meeting link.
>
> Best,
> Lance
>

preview.body: Quess, It was a pleasure speaking with you today. I'm looping in my colleague Lance so the two of you can find a time to connect. Best, Dave
preview.subject: Quess // Lance

subject: Quess // Lance

messageText:
Lance,

My understanding is that you’re not ready to sign yet and want to clarify a
few things first:

   - You feel the scope of the role is closer to founding engineer, given
   how early you’re joining and the level of responsibility.
   - You feel the comp should reflect that and suggest $300K base and 2%
   equity.
   - You want the offer letter to include both the approximate ownership
   percentage and the current number of outstanding shares.
   - You want clarity on what happens if the company is acquired, including
   whether there is a double trigger acceleration clause.
   - While this gets sorted out, you're asking to increase your current pay
   to $170K annualized, rather than the $2K/week current arrangement.

Is this correct? Let me know if I missed anything.

Best,
Dave

On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 3:04 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> I think I want to talk with Josh about the offer when he gets back. In the
> meantime, can I temporarily be put on a contract with a prorated weekly
> salary of $170,000 until I either sign full-time or decline the offer?
> Thanks!
>
> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 11:00 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Sure, no problem.
>>
>> On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 10:58 AM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Can I get the offer expiration extended by 3 weeks? There are some
>>> things I’d like to discuss in person with either you or Josh. Thanks!
>>>
>>> On Tue, May 5, 2026 at 1:33 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Lance - Spoke to Josh. He does not want to share the Plan. Happy to
>>>> talk through any specific questions you have.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>>
>>>> Dave
>>>>

>>>>> Can you send me the grant plan too please?
>>>>>

>>>>>> Ok, no problem.
>>>>>>

>>>>>> wrote:

>>>>>>> busy fixing code, I'll have to push it back 1 day
>>>>>>>

>>>>>>>> Sounds good
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 2:03 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai>
>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Thanks. I’ll read it over carefully tonight and get back to you
>>>>>>>>> tomorrow.

>>>>>>>>> wrote:

>>>>>>>>>> Hey Lance,

>>>>>>>>>> I updated the offer letter to reflect a 5/4 start date and the
>>>>>>>>>> vesting schedule you discussed with Josh. Please let me know if anything

>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Best,

>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> The start date should be today, 5/4.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> And according to Josh and my agreement, I would get 1% equity in
>>>>>>>>>>> the company, vesting over 24 months with an 8-month cliff.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks,
>>>>>>>>>>> Lance
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 9:38 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai>

>>>>>>>>>>>>

>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>>>>>> Dave

messageText:
Update Codex App to version 26.406.40811+ and Codex CLI to version 0.120.0+ to avoid disruption.

( https://chatgpt.com/ )

Hello,

As outlined in our recent blog post ( https://openai.com/index/axios-developer-tool-compromise/ ) , as a precautionary security measure, the OpenAI security team has rotated code signing material for all OpenAI macOS apps. One or more users associated with an API key you administer is using an outdated version of the Codex App. These users will be blocked until they update to Codex App version 26.406.40811 or later and/or Codex CLI version 0.120.0 or later.

Users do not need to reset their OpenAI password or rotate their OpenAI API keys. To confirm, we found no evidence that OpenAI user data was accessed, that our systems were compromised, or that our software was altered. This action was taken out of an abundance of caution. Applications on iOS, Android, and Windows are not affected.

If your organization deploys these applications through device management software, please push the latest versions of:

* Codex App ( https://chatgpt.com/codex/ )
* Codex CLI ( https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli )

If your organization performs macOS application allowlisting, you may need to update your configuration:

* Team ID: 2DC432GLL2 (no change)
* Organization Name: "OpenAI OpCo, LLC" (changed from "OpenAI, L.L.C.")
* Certificate:
SHA-256: 2b940429ce25cf5954b543cc505ad9daa8640c487b686e90e91eb26afd192cce
SHA-1: d6c8b06196ae506c86e7cf40776c07bc5cc7ed62

Thank you for helping keep your team secure. If you have any additional questions please don't hesitate to reach out to security-response@openai.com.

( https://chatgpt.com/ )

OpenAI
1455 3rd Street
San Francisco, CA 94158
Privacy ( https://openai.com/policies/privacy-policy/ ) · Terms ( https://openai.com/terms )
OpenAI © 2015–2026

Sure, no problem.

On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 10:58 AM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Can I get the offer expiration extended by 3 weeks? There are some things
> I’d like to discuss in person with either you or Josh. Thanks!
>
> On Tue, May 5, 2026 at 1:33 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Hi Lance - Spoke to Josh. He does not want to share the Plan. Happy to
>> talk through any specific questions you have.
>>
>> Best,
>>
>> Dave
>>
>> On Tue, May 5, 2026 at 12:33 PM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>
>>> Can you send me the grant plan too please?
>>>
>>> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 11:08 PM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Ok, no problem.
>>>>

>>>>> busy fixing code, I'll have to push it back 1 day
>>>>>

>>>>>>> Thanks. I’ll read it over carefully tonight and get back to you
>>>>>>> tomorrow.

>>>>>>> wrote:

>>>>>>>> Hey Lance,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> I updated the offer letter to reflect a 5/4 start date and the
>>>>>>>> vesting schedule you discussed with Josh. Please let me know if anything
>>>>>>>> else needs to be changed or if you have any questions.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Best,

>>>>>>>>> The start date should be today, 5/4.
>>>>>>>>>

>>>>>>>>> the company, vesting over 24 months with an 8-month cliff.

>>>>>>>>>> Dave

messageText:
Hey Lance,

I updated the offer letter to reflect a 5/4 start date and the vesting
schedule you discussed with Josh. Please let me know if anything else needs
to be changed or if you have any questions.

Best,
Dave

On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 10:25 AM lance mathias <lance@eragon.ai> wrote:

> Thanks Dave!
>
> The start date should be today, 5/4.
>
> And according to Josh and my agreement, I would get 1% equity in the
> company, vesting over 24 months with an 8-month cliff.
>
> Thanks,
> Lance
>
>
> On Mon, May 4, 2026 at 9:38 AM Dave Anderson <dave@eragon.ai> wrote:
>
>> Lance,
>>
>> Please find your offer letter attached. I left the start date blank for
>> now, so let me know what start date you would prefer.
>>
>> Please let me know if you have any questions.
>>
>> Best,
>> Dave
>>
>

subject: Eragon Offer Letter

messageText:
Active authors have reached 50 reviews this month — additional reviews will be billed at $1 per review

Flex usage has kicked in.



Hi there,
Your organization Eragon has authors who have reached their 50 included reviews this month. Additional reviews by these authors
will be billed at $1 per review.
View developer usage across your organization this month.

MANAGE BILLING [https://app.greptile.com/settings/organization/billing/code-review]

link: https://app.greptile.com/settings/organization/billing/code-review
[https://app.greptile.com/settings/organization/billing/code-review]

Happy shipping,
The Greptile Team
